Button.name: Button
Button.description: Versatile button with variants, sizes, icons, and loading states.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-button density override. When null, the button inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, behaviour matches the historical Comfortable default. Only padding / row-height shifts between values — text size + radius are governed by Size.

parameters.name: Href
parameters.description: When set, the button renders as an <a href> styled exactly like a button — the Blazor-idiomatic polymorphism for the common "link that looks like a button" case (#269). All visual props (Variant/Size/icons) still apply; Disabled/IsLoading map to aria-disabled + non-interactive styling since a link can't be natively disabled. Leave null for a normal <button>.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: HTML button type. Defaults to Button — explicitly NOT submit. The HTML spec says <button> inside <form> defaults to type="submit", which would submit the form on every click. Lumeo defaults to a regular button so Buttons don't silently submit EditForm; opt in via Type="Submit".

parameters.name: StopPropagation
parameters.description: Stop click propagation to ancestors. Use this instead of the Razor directive @@onclick:stopPropagation on a Lumeo component — that directive creates an onclick parameter that case-insensitively collides with the OnClick parameter, producing a compile error. The directive only works natively on HTML elements; this parameter is the equivalent for the <button> element underneath.

parameters.name: PreventDefault
parameters.description: Prevent the default click action on the underlying <button> element. Same rationale as StopPropagation — Razor's @@onclick:preventDefault directive collides case-insensitively with the OnClick parameter on components.

parameters.name: PressEffect
parameters.description: Visual feedback when the button is pressed down. Default is None (no press animation — original behaviour).

enums.name: ButtonPressEffect
enums.description: Visual press feedback style for Button.

parameters.description: Accessible name for the button group (exposes it to AT as a named role="group"). Calendar.name: Calendar
Calendar.description: Date picker calendar grid with single, range, and multi-select modes.

parameters.name: IsMultiple
parameters.description: When true the grid selects a set of independent days: clicking a day (or Enter/Space on it) toggles it in/out of Values. Takes precedence over single Value selection; ignored when IsRange is set.

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: The selected days in multiple-selection mode. Pair with ValuesChanged for two-way binding.

parameters.name: DateTooltip
parameters.description: Optional per-date tooltip text. Returned string becomes the day cell's title attribute (native browser tooltip on hover). Return null or empty for no tooltip on that date. Useful for booking calendars: "Booked by John, 14:00–17:00" or "Sold out".

parameters.name: DateBadge
parameters.description: Optional per-date indicator slot. Returns a RenderFragment rendered absolutely positioned in the bottom-right of the day cell — good for status dots, count badges, icons. Return null for no badge on that date. The slot is non-interactive (pointer-events-none) so clicks still hit the day button.

parameters.name: FirstDayOfWeek
parameters.description: The day of the week to render in the leftmost column. When null (default), uses CultureInfo.CurrentCulture.DateTimeFormat.FirstDayOfWeek — so de-DE / fr-FR / etc. start on Monday automatically, en-US on Sunday. Pass an explicit DayOfWeek to override.

parameters.name: SwipeEnabled
parameters.description: When true (default), users can swipe left/right on the day grid to navigate to the next or previous month on touch devices. Vertical page scroll is unaffected (touch-action: pan-y). Set to false to disable swipe navigation.

examples.title: Default
examples.code: <Calendar @bind-Value="_selectedDate" />

examples.title: Date Range Display
examples.code: @if (_checkIn.HasValue) { Check-in: @_checkIn.Value.ToString("MMM d, yyyy") }

examples.title: Pre-selected Date
examples.code:
<Calendar @bind-Value="_date" />

@code {
    private DateOnly? _date = DateOnly.FromDateTime(DateTime.Today);
}

examples.title: Min & Max Date
examples.code:
<Calendar @bind-Value="_date" MinDate="@_minDate" MaxDate="@_maxDate" />

@code {
    private DateOnly? _date;
    private DateOnly _minDate = DateOnly.FromDateTime(DateTime.Today.AddDays(-7));
    private DateOnly _maxDate = DateOnly.FromDateTime(DateTime.Today.AddDays(30));
}

Card.name: Card
Card.description: Flexible container with header, content, and footer slots.

parameters.name: OnClick
parameters.description: Optional click handler. When set, the card becomes a button-role interactive surface with cursor-pointer, focus-visible ring, keyboard activation, and honours PressEffect.

parameters.name: PressEffect
parameters.description: Visual feedback when the card is pressed (only applies when OnClick is bound). Default is None.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Surface style — four tiers, matching the depth ramp of shadcn (bordered → shadow), Radix Themes (ghost|surface|classic) and MudBlazor (Outlined / Elevation): Default — bordered card with the card background (no shadow). Flat — drops the border + card background; a content-only surface that (with OnClick) is the natural primitive for clickable list rows, replacing the Ghost button + !p-0 workaround. Outline — bordered but with a transparent fill, so the page background shows through; for cards layered on a coloured/!card surface. Elevated — bordered card background plus a shadow-sm for a raised, floating feel (shadcn's default Card depth).

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-card density override. Tightens internal padding when rendered inside dense layouts (DataGrid row, dashboard tile grid). Falls back to ancestor DensityScope or Comfortable.

events.name: OnClick
events.description: Optional click handler. When set, the card becomes a button-role interactive surface with cursor-pointer, focus-visible ring, keyboard activation, and honours PressEffect.

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Card>
    <CardHeader>
        <Heading Level="3" Size="lg" Class="leading-none tracking-tight">Notifications</Heading>
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">You have 3 unread messages.</Lumeo.Text>
    </CardHeader>
    <CardContent>
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm">Your recent notifications will appear here.</Lumeo.Text>
    </CardContent>
    <CardFooter>
        <Button Class="w-full">Mark all as read</Button>

</Card>

        <Stack Gap="0" Class="flex-1">

<Card>

        </Flex>
    </CardFooter>
</Card>

Carousel.name: Carousel
Carousel.description: Slide-based content rotator with autoplay and keyboard nav.

parameters.name: Autoplay

parameters.name: AutoplayInterval
parameters.description: Autoplay tick interval in milliseconds. Defaults to 4000.

parameters.name: PauseOnHover
parameters.description: Pause autoplay while the pointer is over the carousel or focus is inside it (so users can read / interact). Defaults to true.

parameters.name: ShowIndicators

Chart.name: Chart
Chart.description: Declarative chart wrapper over ECharts — 30+ types supported.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Declarative children — ChartThreshold and ChartReferenceZone. Other content renders nothing visible.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible text alternative for the chart (WCAG 1.1.1). When set, the canvas host is exposed as role="img" with this aria-label so screen readers announce a summary instead of an empty graphic. Strongly recommended.

parameters.name: AccessibilityLayer
parameters.description: When true (default, matching shadcn's accessibilityLayer), the chart renders a visually-hidden data table built from the series data and makes the canvas host keyboard-focusable with a summarising aria-label, so screen-reader and keyboard users can reach the otherwise opaque <canvas> data. When AriaLabel is not set, a generated summary of the data is used as the label. Set to false to opt out entirely.

parameters.name: MaxAccessibilityRows
parameters.description: Upper bound on the number of data rows the hidden screen-reader table materialises (default DefaultMaxAccessibilityRows). A big series would otherwise emit one hidden <tr> per data point — thousands of rows of DOM bloat that no assistive tech can usefully navigate. Above the cap the table keeps the first N rows plus a single "… and N more data points" row; the host's summarising aria-label is built from the FULL data so its totals stay accurate. Set to 0 (or negative) to disable the cap and render every row.

parameters.name: IsLoading
parameters.description: When true, forces the loading state on top of the chart (consumer is refetching data). Independent of the JS-driven ECharts loading indicator controlled by ShowLoading.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingSkeleton
parameters.description: When true (default), renders the loading state while the chart has not yet rendered for the first time, and whenever IsLoading is true. Set to false to opt out entirely.

parameters.name: SkeletonKind
parameters.description: Shape family for the skeleton. Each chart wrapper presets a sensible default (bars for Bar, pie ring for Pie, etc.) — consumers only override when they want a different silhouette.

parameters.name: SkeletonStyle
parameters.description: Phantom (default) renders the real chart with synthesized placeholder data in a muted palette; ECharts animates the option swap to real data. Silhouette falls back to the legacy SVG overlay skeleton.

parameters.name: PhantomCycleMs
parameters.description: Phantom-mode refresh interval in milliseconds — each tick regenerates the placeholder dataset with a new random seed so bars/lines/slices visibly redistribute while loading. Defaults to 1400ms; clamped to ≥200ms.

parameters.name: PhantomShapeOverride
parameters.description: Explicit override for the phantom layout (category count, series count, stacking, area fill). When null (default), the shape is inferred from the real Option so the phantom mirrors the actual chart structure.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingLabel
parameters.description: When true (default), overlays a small top-right pill containing a spinner and LoadingText on top of the active skeleton — a clear, always-on visual cue that the chart is loading. The pill renders independently of SkeletonStyle so phantom and silhouette both get the same hint. Set to false for bare visuals where the skeleton speaks for itself.

parameters.name: LoadingText
parameters.description: Label rendered next to the spinner inside the loading pill. Set to null or empty to hide the text and show only the spinner. When not set, uses the active locale's "Chart.Loading" string (default: "Loading…").

enums.name: ChartLabelStrategy
enums.description: Strategy for rendering category-axis labels on cartesian charts.

enums.name: ChartSkeletonKind
enums.description: Shape family for ChartSkeleton. Each value renders a different SVG silhouette that matches the shape of an actual chart, so loading states don't cause layout shift or empty whitespace flicker before ECharts mounts.

enums.name: ChartSkeletonStyle
enums.description: How the loading state's visual is presented. Phantom = render the real chart with synthesized data in a muted palette and morph to real data via ECharts' option-change animation (default — feels continuous). Silhouette = fall back to the legacy SVG skeleton overlay. A small "Loading…" pill (spinner + label) is overlaid on TOP of whichever style is chosen via ShowLoadingLabel so users never mistake the cycling phantom data for a bug. The label can be disabled per-chart for bare visuals.

enums.name: ChartThresholdAxis
enums.description: Which axis a threshold is perpendicular to. Horizontal = a yAxis line spanning the chart width; Vertical = an xAxis line spanning the chart height.

parameters.name: Gradient
parameters.description: When true (default) the fill uses a lighter opacity than a solid area. This is the legacy opacity-only behaviour and is unaffected by GradientFill.

parameters.name: GradientFill
parameters.description: Opt into a real vertical gradient fill: each series' area fades from its own (brand/theme) colour at the top to transparent at the bottom, the look used by modern dashboards. Off by default so existing charts are unchanged. Override the stops with GradientStops.

parameters.name: GradientStops
parameters.description: Explicit gradient colour-stops applied to every series' area fill when set (implies GradientFill). Offsets run 0 (top) → 1 (bottom); colours accept theme tokens like var(--color-primary). When null and GradientFill is true, a per-series colour → transparent fade is used.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Declarative annotation children — ChartThreshold and ChartReferenceZone. Forwarded to the underlying Chart.

parameters.name: IsLoading
parameters.description: When true, overlays a shape-aware loading skeleton on top of the chart (consumer is fetching data). Flips back to the real chart once set to false.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingSkeleton
parameters.description: When true (default), renders a shape-aware skeleton while the chart has not yet mounted and whenever IsLoading is true. Set to false to opt out.

parameters.name: SkeletonKind
parameters.description: Shape family for the loading skeleton. Defaults to ChartSkeletonKind.Area for this chart type; override if you want a different silhouette.

parameters.name: SkeletonStyle
parameters.description: Phantom (default) renders the real chart with placeholder data and morphs to real data via ECharts' option-change animation. Silhouette falls back to the legacy SVG skeleton overlay.

parameters.name: PhantomCycleMs
parameters.description: Phantom-mode refresh interval in milliseconds. Lower = faster data cycling while loading. Defaults to 1400.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingLabel
parameters.description: When true (default), shows a small "Loading…" pill in the top-right corner whenever IsLoading is active. Works on top of any SkeletonStyle.

parameters.name: LoadingText
parameters.description: Label rendered next to the spinner inside the loading pill. Set to empty to hide. Defaults to "Loading…".

parameters.name: LabelStrategy
parameters.description: Controls category-axis label rendering. Smart auto-rotates at higher densities; ShowAll forces all horizontal; Auto thins overlapping labels.

parameters.name: LabelRotate
parameters.description: Manual rotation override (degrees) for category-axis labels.

parameters.name: LabelRotate
parameters.description: Manual override for category-label rotation in degrees (e.g. -30, -45). When set, takes precedence over the rotation implied by LabelStrategy.

parameters.name: LineStyle
parameters.description: Line style passed to ECharts — "solid", "dashed", or "dotted". Defaults to "dashed" for visual distinction from data lines.

parameters.name: ChildContent

parameters.name: Class

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes

parameters.description: When true, overlays a shape-aware loading skeleton on top of the chart (consumer is fetching data). Flips back to the real chart once set to false.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingSkeleton
parameters.description: When true (default), renders a shape-aware skeleton while the chart has not yet mounted and whenever IsLoading is true. Set to false to opt out.

parameters.name: SkeletonKind
parameters.description: Shape family for the loading skeleton. Defaults to ChartSkeletonKind.Funnel for this chart type; override if you want a different silhouette.

parameters.name: SkeletonStyle
parameters.description: Phantom (default) renders the real chart with placeholder data and morphs to real data via ECharts' option-change animation. Silhouette falls back to the legacy SVG skeleton overlay.

parameters.name: PhantomCycleMs
parameters.description: Phantom-mode refresh interval in milliseconds. Lower = faster data cycling while loading. Defaults to 1400.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingLabel
parameters.description: When true (default), shows a small "Loading…" pill in the top-right corner whenever IsLoading is active. Works on top of any SkeletonStyle.

parameters.name: LoadingText
parameters.description: Label rendered next to the spinner inside the loading pill. Set to empty to hide. Defaults to "Loading…".

Checkbox.name: Checkbox
Checkbox.description: Binary input with indeterminate state and accessible label.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name used when Label is not set (the checkbox renders without a visible label). Required for WCAG compliance when the checkbox sits alone in a cell or alongside text that isn't linked to it via for / htmlFor.

parameters.name: Animated
parameters.description: When true, the checkmark draws itself in via stroke-dasharray and the box plays a subtle 200ms scale bounce on check. Default is false.

parameters.name: HapticFeedback
parameters.description: When true, triggers a short haptic vibration via navigator.vibrate() each time the checkbox state changes. Silently no-ops on browsers without the Vibration API (e.g. iOS Safari). Default is false.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Value submitted for this checkbox in a native <form> POST when Name is set and the box is checked. Mirrors the native checkbox default of "on".

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Flex Gap="2" Align="center">
    <Checkbox @bind-Checked="_checked" />
    <Label>Accept terms and conditions</Label>
</Flex>

examples.title: With Binding
examples.code:
<Flex Gap="2" Align="center">
    <Checkbox @bind-Checked="_checked" />
    <Label>Enable notifications</Label>
</Flex>

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Optional leading icon slot. Rendered before ChildContent inside an inline-flex items-center shrink-0 wrapper so sized content (e.g. a colour-dot <span>) retains its explicit dimensions. Only active when neither Avatar nor Icon is set.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-chip density override. When null, inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, matches the historical Comfortable default. Only the padding shifts — text size + radius stay governed by Size.

parameters.name: PressEffect
parameters.description: Visual feedback when the chip is pressed (only applies when Clickable is true). Default is None.

examples.title: Clickable
examples.code:
<ChipGroup>
    @foreach (var filter in _filters)
    {
        var f = filter;
        <Chip Clickable="true"
              Variant="@(_active == f ? Chip.ChipVariant.Solid : Chip.ChipVariant.Outline)"
              Color="@(_active == f ? "primary" : null)"
              OnClick="@(() => _active = f)">
            @f
        </Chip>
    }
</ChipGroup>

parameters.name: Language

parameters.name: Highlighter
parameters.description: Pluggable syntax highlighter: (code, language) => safe HTML. Plug in Prism / Shiki / highlight.js (or a server-side highlighter). The delegate MUST return SAFE HTML — escape untrusted source. Core ships no built-in highlighter on purpose (dependency-free), mirroring StreamingText.MarkdownRenderer. Ignored unless Source is set.

CodeEditor.name: CodeEditor
CodeEditor.description: Source-code editor wrapping CodeMirror 6 with on-demand language packs, dark/light/auto theming, and line numbers.

parameters.name: Language
parameters.description: Source language. Currently supported: csharp, javascript, typescript, html, css, json, markdown, xml, sql, python, plaintext. Unknown values fall back to plaintext.

parameters.name: Theme
parameters.description: light | dark | auto (follows page theme via the `dark` class on <html>).

parameters.name: ShowMinimap
parameters.description: Shows a scrollbar-side minimap of the document. CodeMirror 6 has no built-in minimap, so this lazily loads the @@replit/codemirror-minimap extension from EsmBase on first enable. If that import fails (e.g. offline with no self-hosted copy of the package) the editor degrades gracefully — the rest of the editor keeps working, just without a minimap. Toggling at runtime adds/removes the extension without losing selection or scroll position.

parameters.name: EsmBase
parameters.description: Base URL the CodeMirror core, language packs, theme, and minimap are imported from. Defaults to the public esm.sh CDN. Point this at a self-hosted ESM mirror (or a path served by your own app) for air-gapped / offline / strict-CSP deployments — e.g. EsmBase="/_content/MyApp/esm" or EsmBase="https://esm.myco.internal". A trailing slash is optional. When null, falls back to the global window.lumeoCdn.codeMirrorBase override (if set) and then esm.sh.

parameters.name: WordWrap
parameters.description: "off" | "on"

parameters.name: OnChange
parameters.description: Fires alongside ValueChanged; provided for symmetry with Monaco-style APIs.

events.name: OnChange

Combobox.name: Combobox
Combobox.description: Searchable select with filtering, custom values, and grouping.

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Popover open state. Bind via @bind-Open when the Combobox lives inside a parent that re-renders heavily (e.g. an EditForm with active validation) — otherwise a parameter cascade can close the popover mid-click. In the bound (controlled) mode the parent owns state and the popover survives re-renders. When unbound, the component still functions but is vulnerable to the cascade-close race; binding is the recommended pattern.

parameters.name: MaxDisplayTags
parameters.description: Cap on the number of selected-value chips rendered by ComboboxInput in Multiple mode; the rest collapse into a "+N more" indicator. Mirrors Select.MaxDisplayTags.

parameters.name: Clearable
parameters.description: When true, the input shows a clear (×) affordance that resets Value (or Values in Multiple mode) to its empty state. Mirrors Select.Clearable.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Custom CSS classes merged onto the Combobox's wrapper element. Consistent with every other form component per the library's base API contract.

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: Convenience placeholder shown by ComboboxInput when it doesn't set its own Placeholder. Lets consumers write <Combobox Placeholder="…"> instead of threading it through the input child.

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the combobox text input on first render. See Input.AutoFocus — the HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM, this parameter uses FocusElement after the component has mounted.

parameters.name: Items
parameters.description: Optional data source. When set, ComboboxContent renders options from this list instead of ChildContent.

parameters.name: ItemValue
parameters.description: Extracts the string value for each item. Defaults to ToString().

parameters.name: ItemText
parameters.description: Extracts the display label for each item. Defaults to ItemValue when null.

parameters.name: ItemGroup
parameters.description: Optional group key extractor. When set, options are grouped under sticky headers.

parameters.name: ItemDisabled
parameters.description: Optional per-item disabled predicate.

parameters.name: ItemDescription
parameters.description: Optional per-item secondary line (description) extractor. When set and ItemTemplate is null, the default item renderer shows the description in a smaller, muted line below the label — the standard pattern for command pickers (vscode-style "label + helper text").

parameters.name: ItemIcon
parameters.description: Optional per-item leading icon. Receives the raw item object and returns a RenderFragment (typically a single <Icon> or <SvgGlyph>). Rendered before the label when ItemTemplate is null. Skip via returning null per item to mix iconed and non-iconed rows.

parameters.name: ItemTemplate
parameters.description: Optional custom render fragment for an item's content. Receives the raw item object.

parameters.name: Virtualize
parameters.description: When true and Items is set, uses Blazor's Virtualize component for the option list.

parameters.name: ItemSize
parameters.description: Estimated pixel height of each option row used by the Virtualize component. Default 36.

records.name: ComboboxContext
records.signature:
ComboboxContext(
        string? Value, HashSet<string>? Values, string SearchText, bool IsOpen, bool Multiple, bool IsSearchLoading, bool Creatable,
        EventCallback<string> OnSelect, EventCallback<bool> SetOpen, EventCallback<string> OnSearch,
        EventCallback<string> OnCreateItem,
        string WrapperId, string ContentId, string InputId, int FocusedIndex, string? FocusedItemId, string? FocusedItemValue,
        EventCallback<int> SetFocusedIndex,
        Action<string, string> RegisterItem, Action<string> UnregisterItem, int ItemCount,
        bool Invalid, bool Required,
        // Data-bound mode params (null when composition mode)
        IEnumerable<object>? Items,
        Func<object, string> ItemValue,
        Func<object, string>? ItemText,
        Func<object, string?>? ItemGroup,
        Func<object, bool>? ItemDisabled,
        Func<object, string?>? ItemDescription,
        Func<object, RenderFragment?>? ItemIcon,
        RenderFragment<object>? ItemTemplate,
        bool Virtualize,
        float ItemSize)

gotchas: Data-bound mode (the Items parameter) still requires <ComboboxInput /> and <ComboboxContent /> children — a bare <Combobox Items="..." /> with no children renders only an empty wrapper. Map items with ItemValue/ItemText (there is no ItemLabel parameter); items are typed as object, so the Combobox is not generic (no TItem).

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Combobox @bind-Value="_selected" @bind-Open="_isOpen">
    <ComboboxInput Placeholder="Search frameworks..." />
    <ComboboxContent>
        <ComboboxItem Value="blazor">Blazor</ComboboxItem>
        <ComboboxItem Value="react">React</ComboboxItem>
        <ComboboxItem Value="vue">Vue.js</ComboboxItem>
        <ComboboxEmpty>No framework found.</ComboboxEmpty>
    </ComboboxContent>
</Combobox>

        <ComboboxItem Value="Canada">Canada</ComboboxItem>
        <ComboboxItem Value="France">France</ComboboxItem>
        <ComboboxItem Value="United Kingdom">United Kingdom</ComboboxItem>

parameters.name: LabelClass
parameters.description: Extra classes for the group label — theming hook for the header appearance.

parameters.name: Collapsible
parameters.description: When true the header toggles the group open/closed. Collapsed groups hide their items.

parameters.name: DefaultExpanded

parameters.name: GroupSelect
parameters.description: In Multiple mode, render a tri-state checkbox on the header that selects / deselects every descendant item in one click.

parameters.name: Disabled

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the search input on first render via the existing focus interop. Mirrors cmdk, where a bare Command.Input does NOT autofocus but a dialog-hosted command palette does — so this is opt-in (default false): set AutoFocus="true" when hosting the palette in a Dialog/Popover so the user can type immediately on open. The HTML autofocus attribute is a no-op in Blazor WASM, hence the post-render FocusElement hop (see Input.AutoFocus).

parameters.name: Title

parameters.name: Description

parameters.name: ConfirmText

parameters.name: CancelText
parameters.description: Label for the cancel action button. Defaults to "Cancel".

parameters.name: IsDestructive
parameters.description: When true, the confirm button is styled as destructive (red) — for delete / remove / discard actions.

parameters.name: OnConfirm
parameters.description: Fires after the user confirms. Does NOT fire on cancel — listen to OnCancel for that path if needed.

parameters.name: OnCancel
parameters.description: Fires when the user cancels (or closes the dialog without confirming). Optional — most consumers only care about OnConfirm.

parameters.name: Variant

parameters.name: Size

events.name: OnConfirm
events.description: Fires after the user confirms. Does NOT fire on cancel — listen to OnCancel for that path if needed.

events.name: OnCancel

DataGrid.name: DataGrid
DataGrid.description: Enterprise grid: sort, filter, inline edit, multi-level group (client + server), pin, virtualize, export.

parameters.name: Compact
parameters.description: Dense layout: header and body cells drop from px-3 py-2 to px-2 py-1 (and the root text shrinks to text-sm), so the effective row height genuinely decreases — not just the font size. The flag flows to every cell through DataGridContext{TItem}, so toggling it after first render re-pads the grid live. When virtualization is active (see VirtualItemSize) the default row-height estimate is auto-tightened under Compact unless you pass an explicit VirtualItemSize. Default false.

parameters.name: PageSizeOptions
parameters.description: The list of page-size options shown in the rows-per-page selector. Defaults to { 10, 25, 50, 100 }. Set to e.g. { 10, 50, 100, 200, 300 } for larger datasets, or to an empty array to hide the selector entirely.

parameters.name: ShowSearch
parameters.description: Show the global search box in the toolbar. Default true.

parameters.name: ShowColumnChooser
parameters.description: Show the Columns button (visibility/reorder menu). Default true.

parameters.name: ShowExport
parameters.description: Show the Export button and its format dropdown. Default true. Set false to hide export entirely. For fine-grained per-format control see ExportFormats.

parameters.name: ExportFormats
parameters.description: Which export formats appear in the Export dropdown. Default All. Set to e.g. Csv | Excel to hide PDF in WebAssembly where QuestPDF isn't supported. When no flags are set (or ShowExport is false), the Export button is hidden.

parameters.name: VirtualizeThreshold
parameters.description: Item count above which Blazor's <Virtualize> kicks in. Only relevant when ShowPagination is false (or PageSize > this threshold) — paged grids show at most PageSize rows at once and never trigger virtualization. Default 500.

parameters.name: VirtualItemSize
parameters.description: Estimated row height in CSS pixels — used by <Virtualize> to size the scroll spacers. Tune if your rows are taller (dense badges, two-line cells). Default 41px. When Compact is on and this parameter is left at its default, the estimate auto-tightens to CompactVirtualItemSizepx so the scroll spacers match the shorter compact rows; pass an explicit value to override either way.

parameters.name: VirtualOverscanCount
parameters.description: Extra rows rendered above and below the viewport to hide pop-in during fast scrolling. Default 3.

parameters.name: GroupByFields
parameters.description: Ordered list of fields for multi-level grouping. When set (non-empty) this takes precedence over the single GroupBy parameter. Each entry must be a column Field. The grid renders nested group rows with increasing indentation per level; per-level aggregates are shown on each group header row.

parameters.name: ShowGroupPanel
parameters.description: Shows a slim bar above the grid that lists the current grouping levels as removable chips, plus a dropdown to add a new level from any Groupable column. Default false.

parameters.name: GroupPanelText
parameters.description: Placeholder/hint text shown in the group panel when no fields are grouped.

parameters.name: ChildItemsSelector
parameters.description: When set, the grid renders a hierarchical tree-grid: this selector returns a row's child rows (null/empty = leaf). Each parent row gets an expand chevron in the tree column (see TreeColumnField). Distinct from DetailTemplate, which is a flat expand-for-detail fragment. Tree-grid mode and grouping are mutually exclusive — when both are configured, tree-grid mode wins.

parameters.name: TreeGridDefaultExpanded
parameters.description: Whether tree nodes start expanded. Default false.

parameters.name: TreeColumnField
parameters.description: Field of the column that carries the tree chevron + indentation. Null (default) uses the first visible column.

parameters.name: OnTreeNodeExpand
parameters.description: Raised when a tree node is expanded or collapsed (the item, plus its new expanded state).

parameters.name: Expandable
parameters.description: When true, adds a small Expand icon to the toolbar. Clicking it renders the grid in a fullscreen modal overlay. Default false.

parameters.name: IsExpanded
parameters.description: When true, the grid is currently rendered in fullscreen. Bind this for programmatic control, or leave unbound to let the grid manage its own state via the toolbar button.

parameters.name: FullscreenTitle
parameters.description: Optional title shown in the fullscreen header bar. Defaults to "Data Grid".

parameters.name: Virtualized
parameters.description: Enables Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.Virtualization.Virtualize{TItem} with an ItemsProvider — rows are fetched from the backend in scroll-driven windows instead of paged. Requires OnRangeRequest. When active, pagination is hidden automatically and the grid renders a single tall scrollable body.

parameters.name: OnRangeRequest
parameters.description: Range-fetch callback used in virtualised server mode. The grid invokes this whenever the viewport scrolls into an unloaded window. The callback must return the requested slice plus the current total row count so the scroll spacers stay accurate.

parameters.name: OnBatchSave
parameters.description: Fired when the user clicks "Save all" while EditMode is Batch. Args carry the buffered modifications + added rows. On a clean callback return (no exception), the grid's pending-changes buffer is cleared automatically.

parameters.name: ShowAddRow
parameters.description: When true and NewItemFactory is set, renders a thin "+ Add row" trigger below the body. Each click creates a TItem via the factory, pushes it into the added-rows buffer, and immediately shows it inline so cells can be filled in. Only meaningful in Batch.

parameters.name: NewItemFactory
parameters.description: Factory used by the "+ Add row" trigger to produce a fresh TItem. Required when ShowAddRow is true. The returned item is stored in the added-rows buffer; the user fills its cells inline and the row is delivered via OnBatchSave.

parameters.name: ColumnVirtualize
parameters.description: Simplified column virtualization: caps the number of horizontally- rendered data columns at MaxVisibleColumns. Pinned columns always make the cut. Default false.

parameters.name: MaxVisibleColumns
parameters.description: Maximum columns rendered when ColumnVirtualize is true. Default 30.

parameters.name: BatchSaveAllText
parameters.description: Label for the batch-mode "Save all" button. Override for localization.

parameters.name: BatchDiscardText
parameters.description: Label for the batch-mode "Discard" button. Override for localization.

parameters.name: BatchAddRowText
parameters.description: Label for the batch-mode "+ Add row" trigger. Override for localization.

parameters.name: OnColumnResize
parameters.description: Raised when a column is resized through the built-in UI — a pointer drag on the resize handle, a keyboard nudge (arrow keys on the focused handle), or a double-click auto-fit-to-content. The event args carry the column id, the committed pixel width (already clamped to Min/Max), and an AutoFit flag that is true only for the double-click auto-fit path. Column widths persist automatically with the rest of the layout when EnableLayoutPersistence is on; this callback is for consumers that also want to observe/store widths.

parameters.name: SelectionKeySelector
parameters.description: Optional key projector that identifies items across re-fetches. In server-mode where Items is a fresh instance per page-load, the default reference-based stale-selection cleanup wipes selections that survived a page. When this selector is set, the grid identifies stale-vs-still-selected rows by comparing keys instead of references — so a selection on page 1 stays applied after a page-2 fetch returns a fresh wrapper for the same logical row. Returning null from the selector for any item falls back to reference equality for that row.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: CultureInfo used for cell value formatting (dates/numbers) and exports. When null (default), falls back to CurrentCulture. Useful when your app sets a different culture than the UI locale — e.g. force de-DE formatting in an English UI.

events.name: OnTreeNodeExpand
events.description: Raised when a tree node is expanded or collapsed (the item, plus its new expanded state).

events.name: OnBatchSave
events.description: Fired when the user clicks "Save all" while EditMode is Batch. Args carry the buffered modifications + added rows. On a clean callback return (no exception), the grid's pending-changes buffer is cleared automatically.

events.name: OnColumnResize
events.description: Raised when a column is resized through the built-in UI — a pointer drag on the resize handle, a keyboard nudge (arrow keys on the focused handle), or a double-click auto-fit-to-content. The event args carry the column id, the committed pixel width (already clamped to Min/Max), and an AutoFit flag that is true only for the double-click auto-fit path. Column widths persist automatically with the rest of the layout when EnableLayoutPersistence is on; this callback is for consumers that also want to observe/store widths.

enums.name: DataGridExportFormat
enums.description: Flags enum controlling which export formats appear in the DataGrid{TItem} toolbar's Export dropdown. Defaults to All so existing grids keep listing every format. Set to a subset (e.g. Csv | Excel) to hide individual formats — useful in WebAssembly where QuestPDF's PDF export throws PlatformNotSupportedException.

examples.title: Basic DataGrid
examples.code:
<DataGrid TItem="Employee" Items="@employees" PageSize="5" ShowPagination="true">
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Name" Field="Name" Sortable="true" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Department" Field="Department" Sortable="true" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Salary" Field="Salary" Sortable="true" Format="C0" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Start Date" Field="StartDate" Format="yyyy-MM-dd" />
</DataGrid>

examples.title: Column Groups + Footer Aggregates
examples.code:
@* DataGridColumnGroup wraps a contiguous run of column defs under a labelled parent
   <th colspan>. Set Aggregate on a column to surface a sticky footer total; FooterFormat
   formats just the total (cells in C0, totals in N0). *@
<DataGrid TItem="Employee" Items="@employees">
    <DataGridColumnGroup TItem="Employee" Label="Identity">
        <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Name" Field="Name" Sortable="true" />
        <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Department" Field="Department" Sortable="true" />
    </DataGridColumnGroup>
    <DataGridColumnGroup TItem="Employee" Label="Compensation">
        <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Salary" Field="Salary"
                           Sortable="true" Format="C0"
                           Aggregate="AggregateType.Sum" FooterFormat="N0" />
        <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Tenure" Field="StartDate"
                           Sortable="true" Format="yyyy-MM-dd" />
    </DataGridColumnGroup>
</DataGrid>

examples.title: Toolbar, Search & Filtering
examples.code:
<DataGrid TItem="Employee" Items="@employees" PageSize="5"
          ShowToolbar="true" ShowPagination="true">
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Name" Field="Name"
                       Sortable="true" Filterable="true" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Salary" Field="Salary"
                       Filterable="true" FilterType="DataGridFilterType.Number" Format="C0" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Status" Field="Status"
                       Filterable="true" />
</DataGrid>

examples.title: Minimal toolbar (search only)
examples.code:
<DataGrid TItem="Employee" Items="@employees" PageSize="5"
          ShowToolbar="true"
          ShowColumnChooser="false"
          ShowExport="false">
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Name" Field="Name" Sortable="true" Filterable="true" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Department" Field="Department" Sortable="true" Filterable="true" />
    <DataGridColumnDef TItem="Employee" Title="Salary" Field="Salary" Sortable="true" Format="C0" />
</DataGrid>

parameters.name: ServerVirtualizationProvider
parameters.description: When set, switches the body to ItemsProvider-mode virtualisation (window-fetched server scrolling). Null disables.

records.name: IndexedItem
records.signature: IndexedItem(TItem Item, int Index)

parameters.name: RowIndex
parameters.description: 0-based index of the owning row within DisplayedItems (non-grouped).

parameters.name: ColumnIndex
parameters.description: 0-based index of this column within the visible columns.

parameters.name: AriaColIndex
parameters.description: 1-based ARIA column index (includes any leading non-data columns).

parameters.name: Navigable
parameters.description: Whether this cell participates in roving-tabindex keyboard navigation. False in grouped mode where row indices are not globally unique.

parameters.name: FooterFormat
parameters.description: Optional .NET format string for the footer aggregate strip — overrides Format for the totals row only. Useful when the cell format differs from the desired totals format (e.g. cells "C2", totals "N0").

parameters.name: Visible
parameters.description: Two-way bindable column visibility. Seeds the registered column's initial visibility and lets a consumer show/hide the column programmatically via @bind-Visible. When the user toggles the column through the built-in column-chooser, VisibleChanged fires so the bound value follows. Default true.

parameters.name: VisibleChanged
parameters.description: Fires when the column's visibility changes through the built-in UI — the callback half of Visible's two-way binding.

events.name: VisibleChanged
events.description: Fires when the column's visibility changes through the built-in UI — the callback half of Visible's two-way binding.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Header label shown in the spanning th above the grouped columns.

parameters.name: HeaderClass
parameters.description: Extra classes for the spanning header cell — theming hook for the group.

parameters.name: Level
parameters.description: Nesting depth for multi-level grouping (0 = top level). Controls indentation.

parameters.name: Aggregates
parameters.description: Per-group aggregate strip rendered next to the group label.

parameters.name: AriaColIndex
parameters.description: 1-based ARIA column index (includes any leading non-data columns).

parameters.name: PageSizeOptions
parameters.description: Page-size options shown in the rows-per-page selector. Empty array hides the selector entirely.

parameters.name: RowToken
parameters.description: Stable per-slot token for this row (see RowTokenAt) — only the value-type half of RowKey's identity is ever built from this; reference types key off their own instance identity hash instead.

parameters.name: RegisteredContent
parameters.description: The ToolbarContent{TItem} component the consumer placed inside the DataGrid, forwarded by the parent grid. When null, the toolbar's left slot renders just the search + filter chips; when non-null, its Class styles the wrapper and its ChildContent renders alongside the chips.

parameters.name: ShowSearch
parameters.description: Show the global search box. Default true.

parameters.name: ShowColumnChooser
parameters.description: Show the Columns (visibility/reorder) button. Default true.

parameters.name: ShowExport
parameters.description: Show the Export dropdown. Default true. Combined with ExportFormats.

parameters.name: ExportFormats
parameters.description: Which export formats to include in the dropdown. Default All.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Custom CSS classes applied to the wrapper around the toolbar content (the flex row holding your custom buttons + the filter chips).

DataTable.name: DataTable
DataTable.description: Table with sorting, pagination, and row selection built in.

parameters.name: ItemKey
parameters.description: Optional stable-key selector for selection membership. When TItem is a plain reference type (not a record / value type), the default HashSet{T} compares by object reference — so refreshing Items with new instances that are value-equal but reference-distinct silently drops the selection highlight. Supply a key (e.g. p => p.Id) and membership is decided by that key instead of reference identity, so selection survives a same-content refresh. When null, membership falls back to SelectedItems.Contains(item) (reference/value equality), preserving the original behaviour.

parameters.name: AriaColCount
parameters.description: Total column count reported via aria-colcount on the grid (including the selection column when Selectable is on). Set this when the header/row templates render a fixed number of columns so assistive tech can announce "column X of N". 0 (default) omits the attribute since the count can't be inferred from consumer templates.

parameters.name: Virtualize
parameters.description: Virtualize the row list using Blazor's <Virtualize> — only renders rows in the viewport.

parameters.name: ItemSize
parameters.description: Estimated row height in pixels used by the virtualizer (default 41). Only relevant when Virtualize is true.

examples.title: Default
examples.code: Status Email Amount @payment.Status @payment.Email $@payment.Amount

examples.title: Loading State
examples.code: ... ...

<DataTable Items="Array.Empty<Item>()" TItem="Item">
    <HeaderTemplate>...</HeaderTemplate>
    <EmptyTemplate>
        <EmptyState Title="No data" Description="Nothing to display." />
    </EmptyTemplate>
    <RowTemplate>...</RowTemplate>
</DataTable>

examples.code: Name Email Role @context.Name @context.Email @context.Role @code { private string? _sortColumn; private DataTable.SortDirection _sortDir = DataTable.SortDirection.None; private void HandleSort((string Column, DataTable.SortDirection Direction) sort) { _sortColumn = sort.Column; _sortDir = sort.Direction; } }

DatePicker.name: DatePicker
DatePicker.description: Calendar popover for picking a single date or range.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Picker visual style. Defaults to Calendar.

parameters.name: DateTimeValue
parameters.description: Optional DateTime? binding. When set, takes precedence over Value. Changes fire both ValueChanged (DateOnly?) and DateTimeValueChanged (DateTime?). When ShowTimePicker is enabled the current Time selection is folded into the emitted DateTime; otherwise the time component is midnight.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: Date format string. When null (default), the display falls back to the current culture's ShortDatePattern — so a de-DE user sees 15.03.2026 and an en-US user sees 3/15/2026 automatically.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Overrides CurrentCulture for parsing and formatting within this picker. Leave null to honour ASP.NET request localization.

parameters.name: IsDateDisabled
parameters.description: Custom predicate to disable specific dates (e.g. booked rooms, unavailable slots). Returns true for dates that should not be selectable.

parameters.name: DateClass
parameters.description: Custom CSS classes per day cell (e.g. status backgrounds for booking calendars).

parameters.name: DateTooltip
parameters.description: Per-date tooltip text rendered as the day cell's title attribute (native browser tooltip).

parameters.name: DateBadge
parameters.description: Per-date indicator slot (status dots, count badges, icons) rendered absolutely positioned in the bottom-right of the day cell.

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: The selected days for Multiple. Clicking a day (or Enter/Space on it) toggles it in/out of the set; the popover stays open so several days can be picked in one session. Pair with ValuesChanged for two-way binding.

parameters.name: AllowKeyboardInput
parameters.description: When true (default), the trigger renders as a typeable <input> in the configured Format. On blur / Enter the buffer is parsed via DateOnly.TryParseExact(string, string, IFormatProvider, System.Globalization.DateTimeStyles, out DateOnly) (with a fallback to DateOnly.TryParse(string, IFormatProvider, System.Globalization.DateTimeStyles, out DateOnly)). On success the value commits via ValueChanged; on failure the buffer reverts and OnParseError fires. Set to false to keep the historic button-only trigger (input readonly). Has no effect for Range, Wheel, Inline, or when a custom TriggerContent is supplied.

parameters.name: OnParseError
parameters.description: Fires when keyboard input cannot be parsed against the active Format (or culture short pattern), or parses to a date that is rejected by MinDate/MaxDate/IsDateDisabled. The argument is the offending raw buffer.

parameters.name: Inline
parameters.description: Optional controlled-mode override for the popover open state. When non-null, the consumer holds the source of truth — the picker no longer mutates its internal state and instead raises OpenChanged. Use this when the picker lives inside a parent that re-renders heavily (e.g. an EditForm with active DataAnnotationsValidator) and the popover would otherwise close mid-click during a parameter cascade. When null (default), the picker falls back to its internal open state and behaves exactly as before.

parameters.name: FirstDayOfWeek
parameters.description: Leftmost column day of week. Null means culture default.

parameters.name: ShowWeekNumbers
parameters.description: When true, renders an ISO week-number column.

parameters.name: OpenChanged
parameters.description: Fires whenever the popover would open or close. Pair with Open via @bind-Open for the controlled-mode pattern described above.

parameters.name: FullWidth
parameters.description: When true, the date picker expands to fill its container width — equivalent to FullWidth on a Button. Propagates w-full to the Popover wrapper and PopoverTrigger so the constraint reaches the rendered trigger element through the shrink-wrap chain.

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the date-picker trigger on first render. Whichever trigger element is rendered (button / range-button / masked input) receives the focus via its shared id. See Input.AutoFocus for the rationale.

events.name: OnParseError
events.description: Fires when keyboard input cannot be parsed against the active Format (or culture short pattern), or parses to a date that is rejected by MinDate/MaxDate/IsDateDisabled. The argument is the offending raw buffer.

events.name: OpenChanged
events.description: Fires whenever the popover would open or close. Pair with Open via @bind-Open for the controlled-mode pattern described above.

enums.name: DatePickerVariant
enums.description: Visual style of the picker. Calendar renders the existing month-grid Calendar (default). Wheel renders three iOS-style snap-scrolling columns (day / month / year) — designed for mobile / touch UIs.

records.name: DatePickerPreset
records.signature: DatePickerPreset(string Label, DateOnly Date, DateOnly? End = null)
records.description: Quick-select preset. For Single only is used; for Range the preset selects .. (or a single-day range when is null).

examples.title: Default
examples.code: <DatePicker @bind-Value="_selectedDate" />

examples.title: With Label
examples.code:
<Label>Date of birth</Label>
<DatePicker @bind-Value="_date" Placeholder="Pick your date of birth" />

examples.title: Custom Format
examples.code: <DatePicker @bind-Value="_date" Format="yyyy-MM-dd" Placeholder="YYYY-MM-DD" />

examples.title: Min & Max Date
examples.code:
<DatePicker @bind-Value="_date" MinDate="@_minDate" MaxDate="@_maxDate" Placeholder="Select within range" />

@code {
    private DateOnly? _date;
    private DateOnly _minDate = DateOnly.FromDateTime(DateTime.Today);
    private DateOnly _maxDate = DateOnly.FromDateTime(DateTime.Today.AddDays(30));
}

parameters.name: IsDateDisabled
parameters.description: Custom predicate to disable specific dates (e.g. booked rooms). Returns true for dates that should not be selectable.

parameters.name: DateClass
parameters.description: Custom CSS classes per day cell — forwarded to inner Calendar.

parameters.name: DateTooltip
parameters.description: Per-date tooltip text rendered as the day cell's title.

parameters.name: DateBadge
parameters.description: Per-date indicator slot rendered absolutely positioned in the bottom-right of the day cell.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: Date format. When null (default) the inner DatePicker falls back to the effective culture's ShortDatePattern — so a de-DE user sees 15.03.2026 and an en-US user sees 3/15/2026 automatically.

parameters.name: FullWidth
parameters.description: When true, the date range picker expands to fill its container width. Forwarded directly to the inner DatePicker.

DateTimePicker.name: DateTimePicker
DateTimePicker.description: Combined date + time picker with timezone awareness.

parameters.name: OffsetValue
parameters.description: Optional DateTimeOffset binding. When the caller binds this, it takes precedence: writes flow back through OffsetValueChanged with the offset that matches TimeZone at the picked wall-clock time (DST-aware). The plain ValueChanged still fires for back-compat. Recommended over Value when working with multi-zone scenarios.

parameters.name: TimeZone
parameters.description: When non-null, all display/edit happens in this zone — the user picks a wall-clock instant in that zone, and the emitted DateTimeOffset carries the matching offset for that instant (DST-aware). When null, the picker behaves exactly as before (local time, no zone indicator).

parameters.name: ShowTimeZoneLabel
parameters.description: When true (default) and TimeZone is set, renders a small "Europe/Berlin (UTC+1)"-style indicator next to the value.

parameters.name: MinuteStep
parameters.description: Minute column increment. 1 (default) lists every minute; 5/15 etc. list only multiples of the step.

parameters.name: SecondStep
parameters.description: Second column increment (only relevant when ShowSeconds is true). Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: DateFormat
parameters.description: Date portion format. When null (default) falls back to the effective culture's ShortDatePattern.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Overrides CurrentCulture for date/time formatting. Leave null to honour ASP.NET request localization.

parameters.name: FirstDayOfWeek
parameters.description: Override the locale-derived first day of week shown by the embedded calendar. Forwarded to the inner Calendar; leave null to fall back to CurrentCulture.

examples.title: Basic DateTimePicker
examples.code: <DateTimePicker @bind-Value="_dateTime" />

examples.code: <DateTimePicker @bind-Value="_dateTime" Use24Hour="false" ShowSeconds="true" />

examples.title: Min & Max Date
examples.code:
<DateTimePicker @bind-Value="_dateTime" MinDate="@_minDate" MaxDate="@_maxDate" Placeholder="Select within range" />

@code {
    private DateTime? _dateTime;
    private DateTime _minDate = DateTime.Today;
    private DateTime _maxDate = DateTime.Today.AddDays(30);
}

Delta.name: Delta
Delta.description: Trend indicator showing delta value with up/down arrow + color.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Overrides CurrentCulture for number formatting (decimal separator, group separator). Leave null to honour ASP.NET request localization — a de-DE user sees +12,5% while an en-US user sees +12.5%.

Dialog.name: Dialog
Dialog.description: Modal dialog with header, content, footer, and focus trap.

parameters.name: DefaultOpen
parameters.description: Uncontrolled initial open state. Used only when the consumer does NOT bind Open (Radix defaultOpen): the dialog then owns its open state internally, seeded from this value. When Open is supplied the component is controlled and this is ignored.

parameters.name: OnBeforeClose
parameters.description: Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape, backdrop click, X button, or a DialogClose trigger). Set Cancel to true to abort the dismiss — useful for "discard unsaved changes?" guards. Inspect Reason to find out which gesture asked to close.

events.name: OnBeforeClose
events.description: Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape, backdrop click, X button, or a DialogClose trigger). Set Cancel to true to abort the dismiss — useful for "discard unsaved changes?" guards. Inspect Reason to find out which gesture asked to close.

records.name: DialogContext
records.signature: DialogContext(bool IsOpen, EventCallback<bool> SetOpen, string TitleId, string DescriptionId, Func<string, Task<bool>> TryDismiss, DialogDescriptionRegistry DescriptionRegistry = null!, Action RegisterDescription = null!, Action UnregisterDescription = null!)

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Dialog @bind-Open="_isOpen">
    <DialogTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Edit Profile</Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent>
        <DialogHeader>
            <DialogTitle>Edit Profile</DialogTitle>
            <DialogDescription>Make changes to your profile here.</DialogDescription>
        </DialogHeader>
        <Grid Columns="4" Gap="4" Class="items-center py-4">
            <Label Class="text-right">Name</Label>
            <Input Class="col-span-3" Value="John Doe" />
            <Label Class="text-right">Username</Label>
            <Input Class="col-span-3" Value="@johndoe" />
        </Grid>
        <DialogFooter>
            <DialogClose>
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Cancel</Button>
            </DialogClose>
            <DialogClose>
                <Button>Save Changes</Button>
            </DialogClose>
        </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

@code {
    private bool _isOpen;
}

examples.title: Simple Dialog
examples.code:
<Dialog @bind-Open="_isOpen">
    <DialogTrigger>
        <Button>Share</Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent>
        <DialogHeader>
            <DialogTitle>Share this document</DialogTitle>
            <DialogDescription>Anyone with the link can view this document.</DialogDescription>
        </DialogHeader>
        <Stack Gap="4" Class="py-4">
            <Input Value="https://example.com/share/abc123" />
        </Stack>
        <DialogFooter>
            <DialogClose>
                <Button>Copy Link</Button>
            </DialogClose>
        </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

@code {
    private bool _isOpen;
}

examples.title: Confirmation Dialog
examples.code:
<Dialog @bind-Open="_isOpen">
    <DialogTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Destructive">
            <SvgGlyph Svg="@(Lucide.Trash)" class="h-4 w-4" /> Delete Account
        </Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent>
        <DialogHeader>
            <DialogTitle>Are you absolutely sure?</DialogTitle>
            <DialogDescription>
                This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete your
                account and remove your data from our servers.
            </DialogDescription>
        </DialogHeader>
        <DialogFooter>
            <DialogClose>
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Cancel</Button>
            </DialogClose>
            <DialogClose>
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Destructive">Yes, delete account</Button>
            </DialogClose>
        </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

@code {
    private bool _isOpen;
}

examples.title: Prevent Close
examples.code:
<Dialog @bind-Open="_preventCloseOpen">
    <DialogTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Open Protected Dialog</Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent PreventClose="true">
        <DialogHeader>
            <DialogTitle>Unsaved Changes</DialogTitle>
            <DialogDescription>You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave?</DialogDescription>
        </DialogHeader>
        <DialogFooter>
            <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" OnClick="@(() => _preventCloseOpen = false)">Discard</Button>
            <Button OnClick="@(() => _preventCloseOpen = false)">Save Changes</Button>
        </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

@code {
    private bool _preventCloseOpen;
}

parameters.name: ShowCloseButton
parameters.description: Whether the top-end close (X) button renders. null (default) keeps the legacy coupling — X shows whenever PreventClose is false. true forces the X even on a modal dialog (backdrop/Escape stay disabled; the X still routes through the dismiss guard so an OnBeforeClose veto applies); false hides it for fully custom close chrome. Mirrors OverlayOptions.ShowCloseButton for service dialogs.

parameters.name: PlayExitAnimation
parameters.description: When true (the default), closing the dialog keeps the panel mounted for a brief zoom-out (and backdrop fade-out) before unmounting, instead of vanishing instantly — so a declarative <DialogContent> animates its close the same way a declarative Sheet already does, and the same way OverlayProvider already animates service-opened dialogs. Set to false to opt OUT (immediate unmount on close) for tests or consumers who want an instant dismiss.

parameters.name: ZIndex
parameters.description: Backdrop z-index. Content sits at ZIndex + 1. Defaults to BaseZIndex; OverlayProvider supplies an incrementing value per nested overlay so a Dialog opened from inside another Dialog stacks above instead of colliding.

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary markup — receives the TriggerSlot to splat manually. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, renders NO wrapper and cascades a TriggerSlot a cooperating child (a Lumeo <Button>, or a manual ChildContentSlot) folds onto its own single root element — the Radix asChild goal: one DOM node, no nested-interactive markup. Prefer a <button> child (a <Button Href> link activates on Enter only, not Space). Default false keeps the current role=button wrapper.

parameters.name: Direction
parameters.description: Layout direction applied to descendants. Rtl flips logical CSS utilities, native text alignment, form controls and bidi text. Default Ltr.

examples.title: LTR vs RTL
Nested scope override
examples.code:
<DirectionProvider Direction="LayoutDirection.Rtl">
    <Flex Align="center" Gap="3">
        <SvgGlyph Svg="@(Lucide.Info)" class="h-5 w-5 text-primary" />
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm">Outer panel is RTL.</Lumeo.Text>
    </Flex>

    @* Nested provider overrides just this subtree *@
    <DirectionProvider Direction="LayoutDirection.Ltr">
        <Flex Align="center" Gap="3">
            <SvgGlyph Svg="@(Lucide.Info)" class="h-5 w-5 text-primary" />
            <Lumeo.Text Size="sm">This block is forced back to LTR.</Lumeo.Text>
        </Flex>
    </DirectionProvider>
</DirectionProvider>

examples.title: Wrapping the whole app
examples.code:
@* App.razor / MainLayout — wrap once. *@
<DirectionProvider Direction="@(_isRtl ? LayoutDirection.Rtl : LayoutDirection.Ltr)">
    @Body
</DirectionProvider>

parameters.name: OnBeforeClose
parameters.description: Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape, backdrop click, swipe gesture, or a DrawerClose trigger). Set Cancel to true to abort. The Reason tells you which path fired ("escape", "outside", "swipe", "close").

events.name: OnBeforeClose
events.description: Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape, backdrop click, swipe gesture, or a DrawerClose trigger). Set Cancel to true to abort. The Reason tells you which path fired ("escape", "outside", "swipe", "close").

records.name: DrawerContext
records.signature: DrawerContext(bool IsOpen, EventCallback<bool> SetOpen, string TitleId, string DescriptionId, Func<string, Task<bool>> TryDismiss, DrawerDescriptionRegistry DescriptionRegistry = null!, Action RegisterDescription = null!, Action UnregisterDescription = null!)

@code {
    private bool _snapOpen;
    private int _snapIndex = 1;                      // open at 75%
    private double[] _snapPoints = { 0.4, 0.75, 1.0 };
}

parameters.name: SnapPoints
parameters.description: vaul-style snap points: fractional resting heights in ascending order, each 0 < f ≤ 1 (e.g. [0.4, 0.75, 1] = 40% / 75% / fully open). When set on a Top/Bottom drawer, the panel rests at one of these heights, drags between them, and dismisses when flicked/dragged below the lowest. Ignored for Left/Right (which keep plain swipe-to-dismiss). (#218)

parameters.name: ActiveSnapPoint
parameters.description: Index into SnapPoints of the current resting snap. Two-way bindable: updates as the user drags between snaps, and setting it moves the drawer programmatically. -1 (default) opens at the most-open (last) snap. (#218)

parameters.name: PlayExitAnimation
parameters.description: When true (the default), closing keeps the panel mounted for a slide/fade out before unmounting instead of vanishing instantly — so a declarative drawer animates its close the same way OverlayProvider already animates service-opened ones. Set to false to opt OUT (immediate unmount) for tests or consumers who want an instant dismiss. Ignored in snap-point mode (the JS gesture owns the transform).

parameters.name: ZIndex
parameters.description: Backdrop z-index. Content sits at ZIndex + 1. Defaults to BaseZIndex; OverlayProvider supplies an incrementing value per nested overlay.

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary markup — receives the TriggerSlot to splat manually. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, renders NO wrapper and cascades a TriggerSlot a cooperating child (a Lumeo <Button>, or a manual ChildContentSlot) folds onto its own single root element — the Radix asChild goal: one DOM node, no nested-interactive markup. Default false keeps the current role=button wrapper.

parameters.name: Inset
parameters.description: Indents the item by a label's width so it lines up with checkbox/radio items in the same menu (shadcn inset). Adds ps-8 and emits data-inset.

parameters.name: Variant

records.name: DropdownMenuSubContext
records.signature:
DropdownMenuSubContext(
        bool IsOpen,

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary markup — receives the TriggerSlot to splat manually. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, renders NO wrapper and cascades a TriggerSlot (carrying group + data-state so a descendant chevron still rotates, plus the trigger id and menu ARIA) that a cooperating child folds onto its own single root element — the Radix asChild goal. Prefer a <button> child. Default false keeps the current role=button wrapper.

parameters.name: Disabled
parameters.description: When true the trigger ignores clicks and is marked aria-disabled. The live @onclick sits on this outer role="button" element, not the wrapped Button, so disabling only the inner button (as DropdownButton/SplitButton do) previously still let a click on the trigger padding open the menu — this gates that.

FileManager.name: FileManager
FileManager.description: Headless file and folder explorer — folder tree, breadcrumb path, list/grid views, lazy loading, inline rename, context-menu operations.

parameters.name: CurrentPath
parameters.description: The ID of the currently open folder. Null means the root level. Uses Id as the path identifier.

parameters.name: LoadChildren
parameters.description: Called the first time a folder is opened if its Children is null. Return the loaded children.

FileUpload.name: FileUpload
FileUpload.description: Drag-and-drop file dropzone with progress and validation.

parameters.name: ShowFileList
parameters.description: When true (default), renders the internal file card list after selection. Set to false for immediate-upload flows where the app renders its own file list — the internal card with the remove-X will no longer linger after the upload completes. Selection and upload events (OnFilesSelected, OnUpload, OnFileUploaded) still fire normally regardless of this setting. Pair with Reset via @@ref to clear state and allow the same file to be re-picked.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Size of the trigger when Variant="Button". Mirrors ButtonSize so a button-variant FileUpload can match neighbouring buttons in toolbars or compact forms. Has no visual effect on Dropzone or Avatar variants today.

parameters.name: OnUpload
parameters.description: Consumer-supplied upload handler. Called once per file after selection (or after explicit StartUploadAsync if AutoUpload is false). Receives the item, an IProgress{T} for reporting percent 0-100, and a CancellationToken that fires if the user clicks Cancel. Should return the server-assigned URL/id on success, or throw/return null on failure.

parameters.name: AutoUpload
parameters.description: When true (default), uploads begin immediately after files are selected. When false, the user must click the Upload button to start. Requires OnUpload to be set.

parameters.name: MaxConcurrentUploads
parameters.description: Maximum number of OnUpload calls running simultaneously.

parameters.name: ChunkSize
parameters.description: Optional chunk size hint (bytes) for consumers that want to implement chunked/resumable upload inside their OnUpload handler. The component passes this value through ChunkSize but does NOT implement chunking itself.

parameters.name: OnFilesSelected
parameters.description: Fires when files are selected (raw args, kept for back-compat).

parameters.name: OnFileUploaded
parameters.description: Fires each time a file upload succeeds.

parameters.name: OnAllUploaded
parameters.description: Fires when all queued uploads have finished (succeeded, failed, or cancelled).

parameters.name: OnFileFailed
parameters.description: Fires each time a file upload fails.

parameters.name: OnFileRemoved
parameters.description: Fires when a file is removed from the list.

parameters.name: OnFileRejected
parameters.description: Fires when a file is rejected due to type/size/count constraints.

events.name: OnFilesSelected
events.description: Fires when files are selected (raw args, kept for back-compat).

events.name: OnFileUploaded
events.description: Fires each time a file upload succeeds.

events.name: OnAllUploaded
events.description: Fires when all queued uploads have finished (succeeded, failed, or cancelled).

events.name: OnFileFailed
events.description: Fires each time a file upload fails.

events.name: OnFileRemoved
events.description: Fires when a file is removed from the list.

events.name: OnFileRejected
events.description: Fires when a file is rejected due to type/size/count constraints.

examples.title: Default
examples.code: <FileUpload OnFilesSelected="HandleFiles" />

examples.title: Image Upload
examples.code:
<FileUpload
    Label="Upload an image"
    Description="PNG, JPG or GIF up to 10MB"
    Accept="image/*"
    OnFilesSelected="HandleFiles" />

examples.title: Multiple Files
examples.code:
<FileUpload
    Label="Upload documents"
    Description="Select one or more files"
    Multiple="true"
    OnFilesSelected="HandleFiles" />

examples.title: Upload with progress (fake delay)
examples.code:
<FileUpload
    Multiple="true"
    Label="Upload files"
    OnUpload="@SimulateUpload"
    OnFileUploaded="@(item => _uploadedCount++)"
    OnAllUploaded="@(items => _allDoneMsg = $"All {items.Count} file(s) done!")"  />

@code {
    private async Task<string?> SimulateUpload(
        FileUpload.FileUploadItem item,
        IProgress<int> progress,
        CancellationToken ct)
    {
        for (int pct = 0; pct <= 100; pct += 10)
        {
            ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
            progress.Report(pct);
            await Task.Delay(50, ct);
        }
        return $"https://cdn.example.com/{item.Name}";
    }
}

FileViewer.name: FileViewer
FileViewer.description: Universal file preview — auto-detects type from MIME / extension and renders PDF, images, video, audio, Markdown, JSON, CSV, source code (CodeMirror), and plain text inline; unknown types fall back to a download CTA. Pluggable per-kind renderer overrides; auth-aware HttpClient hook.

parameters.name: Kind
parameters.description: Override detection with an explicit kind. Default Auto.

parameters.name: MimeType
parameters.description: Explicit MIME type from your backend (e.g. "application/pdf"). Wins over HEAD and extension detection but loses to an explicit Kind override.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: Human-readable file name. Used for the toolbar label, the download attribute, and the aria-label. Falls back to the last URL path segment when not provided.

parameters.name: AutoHead
parameters.description: Issue a HEAD request to inspect Content-Type when neither Kind nor MimeType is set. Off by default because many CDNs and signed URLs reject HEAD with 405 / 403.

parameters.name: MaxBytes
parameters.description: Cap on the number of bytes fetched for text-based kinds (Markdown, Code, JSON, CSV, Text). Default 10 MB. Excess content is truncated and a one-line notice is appended.

parameters.name: MaxCsvRows
parameters.description: Cap on the number of CSV rows rendered. Default 1000.

parameters.name: ShowToolbar
parameters.description: Show the top toolbar (file name + download). Default true.

parameters.name: ShowDownload
parameters.description: Show the download button in the toolbar. Default true.

parameters.name: ShowFileName
parameters.description: Show the file name in the toolbar. Default true.

parameters.name: HttpClient
parameters.description: Plug a custom HttpClient for the text fetches. Use this to attach a pre-configured Authorization handler instead of mutating the default client. Highest precedence — wins over HttpClientName, DI, and the factory.

parameters.name: HttpClientName
parameters.description: Name of the client to pull from a DI-registered IHttpClientFactory for the text fetches (Markdown/Code/JSON/CSV/Text). When the factory is registered (the common case once AddHttpClient() has been called) this is the recommended way to get a correctly-pooled client and avoid socket exhaustion. Ignored when an explicit HttpClient is supplied. Null/empty uses the factory's default client.

parameters.name: EnableOfficeOnlineViewer
parameters.description: Show an "Open in viewer" action on the Office-document fallback panel that opens the file in the Microsoft Office web viewer. Off by default because it sends the document URL to a Microsoft endpoint and only works for publicly reachable absolute http(s) URLs.

parameters.name: ConfigureRequest
parameters.description: Hook to mutate the outgoing HttpRequestMessage before it's sent — typical use is adding an Authorization header for signed-but-not-presigned URLs.

parameters.name: CustomRenderers
parameters.description: Per-kind renderer overrides. The fragment receives a FileViewerRenderContext with the resolved URL, kind, the fetched text (when applicable) and the display name. When provided for a given kind, the built-in renderer is bypassed for that kind.

parameters.name: LoadingTemplate
parameters.description: Custom loading view. Default is a centered Spinner.

parameters.name: OnKindDetected
parameters.description: Fires once detection settles, with the resolved kind.

parameters.name: OnLoaded
parameters.description: Fires when the file is fully rendered (text fetched, if any).

parameters.name: OnError
parameters.description: Fires on any detection or fetch error, with a message.

events.name: OnKindDetected
events.description: Fires once detection settles, with the resolved kind.

events.name: OnLoaded
events.description: Fires when the file is fully rendered (text fetched, if any).

events.name: OnError
events.description: Fires on any detection or fetch error, with a message.

enums.name: FileKind
enums.description: Kind of content FileViewer renders. Drives which renderer is invoked and which icon / aria-label is shown in the toolbar. Auto is the default and triggers detection from MIME / extension / HEAD content-type; everything else short-circuits detection.

Filter.name: Filter
Filter.description: Composable faceted filter builder with chips.

parameters.name: Filters
parameters.description: Data-driven filter model (#319): when supplied, the bar renders a dismissable FilterPill per descriptor (Field / Operator / Value) and raises OnRemoveFilter on dismiss. The Pills slot still works and renders alongside, so this is purely additive.

records.name: FilterDescriptor
records.signature: FilterDescriptor(string Field, string? Operator = null, string? Value = null)
records.description: A single active filter: a field, an optional comparison operator (e.g. "=", ">", "contains"), and a display value.

examples.title: Filters with various field types
examples.code:
<FilterBar>
    <ChildContent>
        <Flex Gap="2" Align="center">
            <DropdownMenu>
                <DropdownMenuTrigger>
                    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
                        <DynamicIcon Name="SlidersHorizontal" Class="h-3.5 w-3.5 mr-1.5" />
                        Add Filter
                    </Button>
                </DropdownMenuTrigger>
                <DropdownMenuContent>
                    <DropdownMenuItem OnClick="@(() => ToggleField("Status"))">Status</DropdownMenuItem>
                    <DropdownMenuItem OnClick="@(() => ToggleField("Priority"))">Priority</DropdownMenuItem>
                </DropdownMenuContent>
            </DropdownMenu>

            @if (_fields.Contains("Status"))
            {
                <Select Value="@_status" ValueChanged="v => _status = v">
                    <SelectTrigger Class="w-[160px] h-8 text-xs">
                        <Lumeo.Text As="span">@(_status == "" ? "Status" : $"Status: {_status}")</Lumeo.Text>
                    </SelectTrigger>
                    <SelectContent>
                        <SelectItem Value="Active">Active</SelectItem>
                    </SelectContent>
                </Select>
            }
        </Flex>
    </ChildContent>
    <Pills>
        @if (_status != "")
        {
            <FilterPill Label="Status" Value="@_status" OnDismiss="@(() => _status = "")" />
        }
    </Pills>
</FilterBar>
