<FilterBar>
    <ChildContent>
        <Flex Gap="2" Align="center">
            <Input placeholder="Search..." Value="@_search"
                   OnInput="e => _search = e.Value?.ToString() ?? """ Class="h-8 w-[220px] text-xs" />
            <Select Value="@_role" ValueChanged="v => _role = v">
                <SelectTrigger Class="w-[150px] h-8 text-xs">
                    <Lumeo.Text As="span">@(_role == "" ? "Role" : _role)</Lumeo.Text>
                </SelectTrigger>
                <SelectContent>
                    <SelectItem Value="Admin">Admin</SelectItem>
                </SelectContent>
            </Select>
        </Flex>
    </ChildContent>
    <Pills>
        @if (_search != "")
        {
            <FilterPill Label="Search" Value="@_search" OnDismiss="@(() => _search = "")" />
        }
    </Pills>
</FilterBar>

examples.title: Filters with multi-select
examples.code:
<FilterBar>
    <ChildContent>
        <DropdownMenu>
            <DropdownMenuTrigger>
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">
                    Category: @_categories.Count selected
                </Button>
            </DropdownMenuTrigger>
            <DropdownMenuContent>
                @foreach (var cat in _allCategories)
                {
                    <Flex Gap="2" Align="center" Class="px-2 py-1.5">
                        <Checkbox Checked="@_categories.Contains(cat)"
                                  CheckedChanged="@(v => Toggle(cat, v))" />
                        <Lumeo.Text As="span" Size="sm">@cat</Lumeo.Text>
                    </Flex>
                }
            </DropdownMenuContent>
        </DropdownMenu>
    </ChildContent>
    <Pills>
        @foreach (var cat in _categories)
        {
            <FilterPill Label="Category" Value="@cat"
                        OnDismiss="@(() => _categories.Remove(cat))" />
        }
    </Pills>
</FilterBar>

Form.name: Form
Form.description: EditForm wrapper with styled validation, field groups, and submit state.

examples.title: Input with Validation
examples.code:
<FormField Name="email" Label="Email" Required="true" Error="@_emailError" HelpText="We will never share your email.">
    <Input Type="email" Placeholder="you@example.com" @bind-Value="_emailValue" />
</FormField>
<Button OnClick="ValidateEmail">Validate</Button>

@code {
    private string? _emailValue;
    private string? _emailError;

    private void ValidateEmail()
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_emailValue) || !_emailValue.Contains('@'))
            _emailError = "Please enter a valid email address.";
        else
            _emailError = null;
    }
}

examples.title: Select with Validation
examples.code:
<FormField Name="role" Label="Role" Required="true" Error="@_roleError">
    <Select @bind-Value="_roleValue">
        <SelectTrigger>
            <Text As="span" Class="@(_roleValue == null ? \"text-muted-foreground\" : "")">
                @(_roleValue ?? "Select a role")
            </Text>
        </SelectTrigger>
        <SelectContent>
            <SelectItem Value="admin">Admin</SelectItem>
            <SelectItem Value="editor">Editor</SelectItem>
            <SelectItem Value="viewer">Viewer</SelectItem>
        </SelectContent>
    </Select>
</FormField>
<Button OnClick="ValidateRole">Validate</Button>

@code {
    private string? _roleValue;
    private string? _roleError;

    private void ValidateRole()
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(_roleValue))
            _roleError = "Please select a role.";
        else
            _roleError = null;
    }
}

examples.title: Checkbox and Switch with Validation
examples.code:
<FormField Name="terms" Error="@_termsError">
    <Flex Gap="2" Align="center">
        <Checkbox @bind-Checked="_termsChecked" />
        <Label>I accept the terms and conditions</Label>
    </Flex>
</FormField>
<FormField Name="notifications" Label="Email Notifications" HelpText="Receive updates about your account.">
    <Switch @bind-Checked="_notifChecked" />
</FormField>
<Button OnClick="ValidateTerms">Submit</Button>

@code {
    private bool _termsChecked;
    private bool _notifChecked = true;
    private string? _termsError;

    private void ValidateTerms()
    {
        _termsError = _termsChecked ? null : "You must accept the terms to continue.";
    }
}

parameters.name: AutoRenderMessage
parameters.description: When true (default — the simple-mode API used by the vast majority of consumers), FormField renders the validation error itself below the control. Set false when you compose the field with a child FormMessage that should own the error rendering: this suppresses FormField's own error so the two never render it twice. The error still flows into the cascaded context either way, so a deferred FormMessage can pick it up.

parameters.name: AsyncValidator
parameters.description: Optional async validator. Receives the current Value, returns null when valid or an error message string otherwise. The result is stored in Errors against Name.

parameters.name: AsyncValidationDebounceMs
parameters.description: Debounce window in ms. 0 disables debouncing.

parameters.name: AsyncValidateOn
parameters.description: When async validation runs. Defaults to OnBlur.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Current value of the bound input. Required for OnChange auto-triggering; also passed to the validator on blur. If unset, consumers may invoke ValidateAsync(object?) directly.

parameters.name: OnValidatingChanged
parameters.description: Fired whenever IsValidating flips.

events.name: OnValidatingChanged
events.description: Fired whenever IsValidating flips.

records.name: FormFieldContext
records.signature: FormFieldContext(string? Name, string? Error, bool IsValidating, string ControlId = "", string? DescribedBy = null, bool AutoRenderMessage = true, string? ErrorId = null, string? LabelId = null)

parameters.name: ViewModeChanged
parameters.description: Raised when the user picks a new zoom level from the toolbar. Bind it (@bind-ViewMode) to keep the parent's ViewMode in lock-step with the toolbar; without a binding the component still tracks the toolbar selection on its own (uncontrolled usage).

parameters.name: ZoomLevels
parameters.description: Restricts which zoom buttons the toolbar renders, in the given order. When null or empty the full default set (Day, Week, Month, Year) is shown — matching the previous hardcoded toolbar. Only toolbar-representable levels are drawn: QuarterDay / HalfDay have no toolbar button and are silently skipped here (still settable via ViewMode). Example: ZoomLevels="new[]{ GanttViewMode.Day, GanttViewMode.Week, GanttViewMode.Month }".

parameters.name: DefaultZoom
parameters.description: The zoom level selected on first render. When set it seeds the initial toolbar selection (and the level the timeline mounts at), overriding ViewMode's default — handy for uncontrolled usage, e.g. ZoomLevels="…" DefaultZoom="GanttViewMode.Week". After mount, ViewMode / @bind-ViewMode govern as before. Null falls back to ViewMode (the existing behaviour).

parameters.name: TodayHighlight
parameters.description: Highlight today with a vertical line and dot marker. Default true.

parameters.name: BarHeight
parameters.description: Override the pixel height of each task bar (default 22).

parameters.name: ColumnWidth
parameters.description: Override the pixel width of each date column for the current view mode.

parameters.name: BarColor
parameters.description: Per-task bar colour override. Return a CSS colour string (hex, rgb, or a CSS variable reference like "var(--color-destructive)") or null to use the primary theme colour.

parameters.name: GroupBy
parameters.description: Optional grouping function. When set, tasks are sorted by group label and a visual section-header row is injected before the first task in each group.

events.name: ViewModeChanged
events.description: Raised when the user picks a new zoom level from the toolbar. Bind it (@bind-ViewMode) to keep the parent's ViewMode in lock-step with the toolbar; without a binding the component still tracks the toolbar selection on its own (uncontrolled usage).

enums.name: GanttViewMode
enums.description: Gantt timeline zoom level. Supported by the Lumeo SVG engine: QuarterDay = 6-hour columns, HalfDay = 12-hour columns, Day = 1 column/day, Week = 1 column/week, Month = 1 column/month, Year = 1 column/year.

        new("design",      "Design",      DateTime.Today.AddDays(5),   DateTime.Today.AddDays(12), Dependencies: new[] { "research" }),

        new("testing",     "Testing",     DateTime.Today.AddDays(22),  DateTime.Today.AddDays(30), Dependencies: new[] { "development" }),

<Gantt @bind-Tasks="_tasks"
       ViewMode="GanttViewMode.Day"
       OnDateChange="HandleDateChange"
       OnProgressChange="HandleProgressChange" />

@code {
    private IEnumerable<GanttTask> _tasks = new List<GanttTask> { /* ... */ };
    private GanttTask? _last;

    Task HandleDateChange(GanttTask t)     { _last = t; StateHasChanged(); return Task.CompletedTask; }
    Task HandleProgressChange(GanttTask t) { _last = t; StateHasChanged(); return Task.CompletedTask; }
}

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" and wire @onmouseenter="slot.OnMouseEnter.InvokeAsync" (etc.) onto their own single element. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, the trigger renders NO wrapper element and instead cascades a TriggerSlot that a cooperating child folds onto its own single root element — yielding ONE DOM node with the hover/focus open-close handlers, the pin toggle and aria-expanded forwarded (the Radix asChild goal). The child must resolve to exactly one cooperating element (a Lumeo <Button>, or a manual ChildContentSlot splat). Default false preserves the current inline-flex wrapper exactly.

Icon.name: Icon
Icon.description: Icon wrapper — renders the built-in LumeoIcons set (Lucide-derived) or any IconSource natively.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Lucide icon name (PascalCase, e.g. "Search", "ChevronDown", "TrendingUp"). Unknown names fall back to a Circle. Use Svg to supply any icon directly (any IconSource, e.g. from a Lumeo icon pack).

parameters.name: Svg
parameters.description: Direct IconSource reference — takes precedence over Name. Lets consumers supply any vendored icon (e.g. LumeoIcons.Search or an icon-pack member).

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Size preset. Sets Tailwind h-{n} w-{n} on the SVG. Defaults to Md (h-4 w-4).

parameters.name: StrokeWidth
parameters.description: Overrides the IconSource's stroke width on the rendered svg. Only affects Stroke-style icons (Lucide, Tabler); ignored for Fill sets (Phosphor, Material, Bootstrap) where stroke is not used. When null (default) the icon source's own StrokeWidth is used.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Accessible name for the icon. When set, the icon is exposed to assistive technology as role="img" with this string as its aria-label (and a <title> for tooltip/hover parity). When left null the icon is treated as decorative and hidden from screen readers via aria-hidden="true" — the correct default for icons that sit beside a visible text label (a button's own text already conveys the meaning). A consumer-supplied role/aria-label/aria-hidden/ aria-labelledby in attributes always wins over these defaults.

parameters.name: Svg
parameters.description: The icon to render. Its Content becomes the inner markup and its ViewBox/RenderStyle/ StrokeWidth style the root <svg>.

parameters.name: StrokeWidth
parameters.description: Optional root stroke-width override for Stroke icons. When null (default) the icon's own StrokeWidth is used; ignored for Fill icons.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Arbitrary attributes (class, aria-*, role, title, …) splatted onto the root <svg>. Sizing is done here via classes (e.g. h-4 w-4), exactly like the icon component it replaces. A consumer-supplied attribute that collides with one this component sets (e.g. fill) wins, because the splat is applied last.

Image.name: Image
Image.description: Image with lazy-loading, loading skeleton, and error fallback.

parameters.name: Zoomable
parameters.description: When true, registers a two-finger pinch-zoom gesture on the image container so touch users can zoom in/out on the image directly (independent of the fullscreen Preview overlay). Sets touch-action: none on the wrapper so the browser doesn't intercept the gesture for native page zoom.

Input.name: Input
Input.description: Styled text input with label, prefix/suffix, icons, error state.

parameters.name: ShowCount
parameters.description: Renders a character counter below the input, matching Textarea's pattern.

parameters.name: MaxLength
parameters.description: HTML maxlength on the input plus the limit shown in the counter. When null and ShowCount is true, only the current length is rendered.

parameters.name: CountFormat
parameters.description: Optional custom counter formatter (e.g. c => $"{c} of 60 chars"). Default renders 42 or 42/60 when MaxLength is set.

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the underlying <input> on first render. The HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM because the browser's autofocus algorithm runs during initial document parsing — at parse time the WASM runtime hasn't booted yet and no <input> exists for it to focus. This parameter uses ElementReference.FocusAsync in OnAfterRenderAsync(firstRender) which is the canonical workaround.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual / semantic input variant. Default is the plain text input. Search auto-renders a search icon prefix, sets type="search" (gives platforms a clear-button + IME hints), and applies a search-friendly minimum width so the field doesn't collapse in narrow flex containers — replaces the min-w-[180px] max-w-md + manual icon prefix consumers previously shipped at every search field call-site.

examples.title: Default

<Input Value="@_name" OnInput="@(e => _name = e.Value?.ToString() ?? "")" placeholder="Enter your name" />
<Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Value: @_name</Lumeo.Text>

@code {
    private string _name = "";
}

parameters.name: OnCardDrop
parameters.description: Raised when a card is dropped onto a column (mouse/touch drag) and the board can resolve which card moved, from which column, to which column and at what index. Fires only when cards carry a CardId and columns a ColumnId; otherwise the per-column OnDrop still fires with the raw event for fully-manual boards.

parameters.name: OnCardMove
parameters.description: Raised when a card is moved via the keyboard (arrow keys on a focused card): Left/Right move it to the previous/next column, Up/Down reorder it within its column. Provides the keyboard-accessible alternative to drag-and-drop. Requires CardId on cards and ColumnId on columns so the board can resolve the move.

events.name: OnCardDrop
events.description: Raised when a card is dropped onto a column (mouse/touch drag) and the board can resolve which card moved, from which column, to which column and at what index. Fires only when cards carry a CardId and columns a ColumnId; otherwise the per-column OnDrop still fires with the raw event for fully-manual boards.

events.name: OnCardMove
events.description: Raised when a card is moved via the keyboard (arrow keys on a focused card): Left/Right move it to the previous/next column, Up/Down reorder it within its column. Provides the keyboard-accessible alternative to drag-and-drop. Requires CardId on cards and ColumnId on columns so the board can resolve the move.

enums.name: MoveDirection
enums.description: The direction of a keyboard-initiated card move.

records.name: DropEventArgs
records.signature: DropEventArgs(string CardId, string? FromColumnId, string ToColumnId, int ToIndex)
records.description: Payload describing a drag-drop of a card between/within columns.

records.name: MoveEventArgs
records.signature: MoveEventArgs(string CardId, string FromColumnId, MoveDirection Direction)
records.description: Payload describing a keyboard-initiated card move.

records.name: BoardContext
records.signature: BoardContext
records.description: A single stable instance cascaded per board. Tracks the in-flight drag so a column's drop handler can read which card is being dragged and from where — the HTML5 DataTransfer is unreliable (and empty under the touch shim), so the board carries the drag identity itself.

examples.title: Drag & Drop Board
examples.code:
<Kanban>
    <KanbanColumn Title="To Do" Badge="3">
        <KanbanCard Title="Design landing page"
                    Labels="@(new List<KanbanCard.KanbanLabel> { new() { Text = "Design", Color = "primary" } })" />
        <KanbanCard Title="Write API docs"
                    Labels="@(new List<KanbanCard.KanbanLabel> { new() { Text = "Docs", Color = "secondary" } })" />
    </KanbanColumn>
    <KanbanColumn Title="In Progress" Badge="1">
        <KanbanCard Title="Build auth flow"
                    Description="Login and register flow."
                    Labels="@(new List<KanbanCard.KanbanLabel> { new() { Text = "High", Color = "destructive" } })" />
    </KanbanColumn>
    <KanbanColumn Title="Done" Badge="1">
        <KanbanCard Title="Project setup" Labels="@(new List<KanbanCard.KanbanLabel> { new() { Text = "Done", Color = "success" } })" />
    </KanbanColumn>
</Kanban>

            <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm font-medium">Sprint 4</Lumeo.Text>
            <Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Secondary">5</Badge>
        </Flex>
    </HeaderContent>
    <KanbanCard Title="Task A" />
</KanbanColumn>

parameters.name: CardId
parameters.description: Stable id for this card, written into the board's drop/move payloads.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: This card's position within its column. Optional: when left unset the column derives the insert index from the card's sibling order at drop time, so the documented usage (which omits Index) still produces a precise insert index instead of always targeting position 0.

parameters.name: OnDrop
parameters.description: Raw drop event. Still fired for fully-manual boards that track drag state themselves. For an enriched payload (which card, from/to column, index) give the column a ColumnId, give cards a CardId, and handle Kanban.OnCardDrop at the board level.

parameters.name: ColumnId
parameters.description: Stable id for this column, written into the board's drop/move payloads.

events.name: OnDrop
events.description: Raw drop event. Still fired for fully-manual boards that track drag state themselves. For an enriched payload (which card, from/to column, index) give the column a ColumnId, give cards a CardId, and handle Kanban.OnCardDrop at the board level.

records.name: ColumnContext
records.signature: ColumnContext
records.description: Cascaded to child cards so a card can advertise the insert index when it is the drag-over target.

Map.name: Map
Map.description: Interactive geographic map powered by MapLibre GL — markers, polylines, polygons, circles, arcs, heatmaps, legend overlays, and popups; CARTO vector basemaps, no API key required.

parameters.name: TileLayer
parameters.description: Accepts a CARTO style preset (Positron, DarkMatter, Voyager), a legacy raster name (OpenStreetMap, CartoLight, CartoDark, Satellite, Terrain) — kept as aliases pointing at the closest GL vector style — or a raw MapLibre style.json URL. Auto (the default) picks Positron or DarkMatter based on the host page's theme, matching mapcn.dev.

parameters.name: Layers
parameters.description: When set, renders a chip-style switcher in the top-right that lets the user pick between presets (or custom { name, url } records).

parameters.name: Cluster
parameters.description: Enable MapLibre GL native marker clustering. When true, markers are aggregated into circle bubbles via a GeoJSON cluster source — no additional script is fetched. Cluster bubbles are tinted with --lumeo-map-cluster-highlight (default amber) when at least one child marker is Highlighted. Fine-tune with ClusterRadius, ClusterMaxZoom, ClusterProperties, and ClusterColorExpression.

parameters.name: ClusterProperties
parameters.description: Additional MapLibre clusterProperties expressions merged into the GeoJSON cluster source alongside the built-in highlighted aggregator. Each entry maps a property name to a raw [operator, expression] array — e.g. { "totalScore", new object[] { "+", new object[] { "get", "score" } } }. Only meaningful when Cluster is true. Built-in keys (highlighted) win on collision.

parameters.name: ClusterColorExpression
parameters.description: Replaces the default circle-color MapLibre paint expression on cluster circles. The default expression tints any bubble that contains a highlighted marker with --lumeo-map-cluster-highlight (default amber). Pass a raw MapLibre expression array to drive cluster color from a custom aggregated property instead.

parameters.name: ClusterRadius
parameters.description: Target pixel radius of each cluster circle. Default is 50.

parameters.name: ClusterMaxZoom
parameters.description: Zoom level at which markers fully emerge from their cluster. Default is 14. Lower values break clusters apart at coarser zoom levels.

parameters.name: Geolocate
parameters.description: Adds a "My location" button that geolocates the user.

parameters.name: Fullscreen
parameters.description: Adds a fullscreen toggle button.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.description: Adds a metric scale bar in the bottom-left corner.

parameters.name: Search
parameters.description: Adds a Nominatim-backed search bar in the top-left.

parameters.name: Projection
parameters.description: MapLibre projection name. "mercator" (default flat web-map) or "globe" for a 3-D spherical view. The globe becomes visible at zoom levels roughly 0–3; it blends back to mercator above zoom 4.

parameters.name: AutoFitBounds
parameters.description: When true the map automatically fits its viewport to include all registered markers and shapes after the first render.

parameters.name: Overlay
parameters.description: Optional content rendered as an absolutely-positioned overlay inside the map container (z-index 10, pointer-events none on the wrapper so map interaction is not blocked). Use absolute-position classes on your own child element to place chips, legends, or status badges (e.g. class="absolute bottom-2 right-2 pointer-events-auto …").

records.name: MapContext
records.signature: MapContext(Map Owner)
records.description: Cascading context passed to MapMarker + shape children so they can register themselves with the parent Map.

gotchas: MapLibre GL JS and its stylesheet are loaded at runtime from unpkg (CDN) the first time a Map mounts. For strict-CSP or air-gapped deployments, self-host them and set window.lumeoCdn = { mapLibreJs: '/your/maplibre-gl.js', mapLibreCss: '/your/maplibre-gl.css' } before the first Map renders, or run `lumeo deps install --lib maplibre` (CLI). See /docs/cdn-deps.

parameters.name: Curvature
parameters.description: How tall the arc rises above the straight chord, as a fraction of the chord's length. 0 = straight line, 1 = a full semicircle-ish bulge. Defaults to 0.25.

parameters.name: DashArray
parameters.description: SVG dash pattern. Defaults to "5 6" (arcs are dashed by default).

parameters.name: Dashed
parameters.description: Set to false to draw the arc as a solid line instead of the default dashed style. Has no effect when DashArray is set explicitly.

parameters.name: HoverColor
parameters.description: Stroke color on hover. Null = no hover highlight.

parameters.name: HoverWeight
parameters.description: Stroke width on hover. Null = same as Weight.

parameters.name: Animate
parameters.description: Animate the arc drawing on first render (line draws from start to end over ~1 s).

parameters.name: Center
parameters.description: Circle center as (lat, lon).

parameters.name: RadiusMeters
parameters.description: Radius in meters. Leaflet draws the circle in real-world units, so its pixel size grows/shrinks with zoom.

parameters.name: Points
parameters.type: IEnumerable<(double Lat, double Lon, double Intensity)>
parameters.description: Collection of heat points. Each tuple is (lat, lon, intensity) where intensity should be in the range 0–1 (defaults to 1 when not meaningful). Pass a bare (lat, lon) list via a helper or use the convenience constructor that sets intensity to 1.

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: Radius of each point in pixels at full zoom. Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: Opacity
parameters.description: Opacity of the heatmap layer (0–1). Defaults to 0.8.

parameters.name: ColorRamp
parameters.description: Color ramp expressed as a MapLibre interpolation stops list. Format: alternating stop (0–1) and CSS color values. Example: new object[] { 0, "transparent", 0.4, "blue", 0.8, "red", 1, "orange" }. Defaults to a blue → cyan → yellow → orange → red ramp.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Optional title shown above the legend items.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.description: Absolute-position corner shorthand. Defaults to "bottom-left". Values: "top-left", "top-right", "bottom-left", "bottom-right". Can also pass a raw Tailwind class via Class.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Dot color. Friendly names ("red", "green", etc.) or any CSS color value.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Label text shown next to the dot.

parameters.name: IconUrl
parameters.description: Bitmap icon URL. When set, takes precedence over Variant — we hand Leaflet a plain L.icon and skip the DivIcon path entirely. Anchored bottom-center at 32×32, matching the pre-3.1 default.

parameters.name: Variant
parameters.description: Visual variant for the auto-generated DivIcon. Ignored when IconUrl is set. Defaults to "Default" (filled circle). Valid values: "Default", "Outline", "Pin", "Dot".

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Override the marker's theme color. Accepts a friendly name ("red", "green", "yellow", …) or a raw CSS color ("#ff0000", "hsl(...)"). Null = theme primary.

parameters.name: Highlighted
parameters.description: Marks this marker as "highlighted" (e.g. a recommendation). The pin itself is unchanged, but with Cluster on, any cluster bubble that contains at least one highlighted marker is tinted with the cluster-highlight color (the --lumeo-map-cluster-highlight CSS variable, default amber) — so a recommendation stays discoverable even while its marker is clustered away.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Permanent label rendered above the marker. Use for waypoints or numbered tours. Different from Title (hover tooltip).

parameters.name: LabelHoverOnly
parameters.description: When true, the Label is hidden by default and only appears on hover. Useful for dense maps where always-on labels create visual noise. Default is false (label always visible).

parameters.name: Icon
parameters.description: Optional inline content inside the marker circle — a single letter, a number, or a small <SvgGlyph> SVG. The fragment is rendered to a hidden host and its innerHTML is spliced into the DivIcon. Ignored when IconUrl is set.

parameters.name: Popup
parameters.description: Optional rich popup body. When present, it overrides the plain Title as the marker's popup content. The fragment renders into a hidden host div and Leaflet uses the host's innerHTML.

parameters.name: Draggable
parameters.description: Allow the user to drag the marker to a new position.

parameters.name: OnDragEnd
parameters.description: Fires with the new (lat, lon) after the user finishes dragging the marker. Use with two-way binding or to update your own state. Only fires when Draggable is true.

parameters.name: ClusterExclude
parameters.description: When true and the parent Map has Cluster enabled, this marker is rendered as a DOM element rather than being absorbed into the cluster source. Use for permanently-visible markers (e.g. a depot, a selected result) that must never be hidden inside a cluster bubble. Default is false.

parameters.name: Properties
parameters.description: Arbitrary key-value properties merged into this marker's GeoJSON feature when Cluster is enabled. Drives custom ClusterProperties aggregation expressions (e.g. a score field summed across the cluster). Built-in keys (id, highlighted, color, hasClick) win on collision.

events.name: OnDragEnd
events.description: Fires with the new (lat, lon) after the user finishes dragging the marker. Use with two-way binding or to update your own state. Only fires when Draggable is true.

parameters.name: Points
parameters.description: Ordered list of (lat, lon) points forming the polygon ring. The first and last points are connected automatically.

parameters.name: Points
parameters.description: Ordered list of (lat, lon) points the polyline visits.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: Stroke color. Friendly name ("red", "blue", …) or a raw CSS color. Null = theme primary.

parameters.name: Weight
parameters.description: Stroke width in pixels.

parameters.name: Opacity
parameters.description: Stroke opacity, 0..1.

parameters.name: DashArray
parameters.description: SVG dash pattern (e.g. "6 4" for dashed). Null = solid.

parameters.name: Dashed
parameters.description: Convenience toggle — renders a standard "6 4" dash when true. Ignored when DashArray is set explicitly.

parameters.name: HoverColor
parameters.description: Stroke color on hover. Null = no hover highlight.

parameters.name: HoverWeight
parameters.description: Stroke width on hover. Null = same as Weight.

parameters.name: Animate
parameters.description: Animate the line drawing on first render (draws from start to end over ~1 s).

parameters.name: Tooltip
parameters.description: Optional hover tooltip text.

parameters.name: PopupHtml
parameters.description: Optional popup HTML (rendered on click).

parameters.name: Lat
parameters.description: Latitude of the popup anchor point.

parameters.name: Lon
parameters.description: Longitude of the popup anchor point.

parameters.name: IsOpen
parameters.description: Whether the popup is currently visible.

parameters.name: CloseButton
parameters.description: Show a close (×) button inside the popup.

parameters.name: CloseOnClick
parameters.description: Close the popup when the user clicks the map background.

parameters.name: Html
parameters.description: HTML string for the popup body. Overrides ChildContent.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Blazor render fragment for rich popup body. Rendered into a hidden host element; JS reads the innerHTML to open the MapLibre popup.

NavigationMenu.name: NavigationMenu
NavigationMenu.description: Top-level site nav with animated dropdown panels.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Accessible name for the <nav> landmark. Defaults to "Main".

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Controlled value of the currently-open item. Bind with @bind-Value/ValueChanged. The value matches the open Value (or its auto id when none is set); null means no submenu is open. Leave unset for uncontrolled use.

parameters.name: ValueChanged
parameters.description: Raised when the open item changes (user hover/click/keyboard, or an item close). Carries the new open item's value, or null when closed.

parameters.name: DefaultValue
parameters.description: Initial open item for uncontrolled use (ignored once Value is bound). null (default) opens nothing.

parameters.name: DelayDuration
parameters.description: Grace window in milliseconds before a hovered submenu closes after the pointer leaves it. Radix's forgiveness delay: moving between adjacent triggers, or briefly slipping off the panel, does not immediately dismiss. Defaults to 150ms.

events.name: ValueChanged
events.description: Raised when the open item changes (user hover/click/keyboard, or an item close). Carries the new open item's value, or null when closed.

records.name: NavigationMenuContext
records.signature: NavigationMenuContext(string? ActiveItemId, EventCallback<string?> SetActiveItemId, NavigationMenu Owner, int CloseDelayMs)

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Stable identity for this item, used by the controlled Value binding to say which item is open. When unset an auto id is used, so uncontrolled menus keep working unchanged.

records.name: NavigationMenuItemContext
records.signature: NavigationMenuItemContext(string ItemId, string TriggerId, string ContentId)

parameters.name: IsActive
parameters.description: When true, the link is the user's current location and is rendered with aria-current="page". Lets screen-reader users identify the active item in a nav landmark.

NumberInput.name: NumberInput
NumberInput.description: Numeric input with stepper buttons and locale formatting.

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Culture used to parse typed input. Display of the value inside the native <input type="number"> element uses InvariantCulture because HTML5 requires period-as-decimal — browsers will not accept a comma decimal in this input type. Leave null to honour ASP.NET request localization for parsing (lets a de-DE user paste 1,5 and have it round-trip to 1.5).

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the underlying number <input> on first render. See Input.AutoFocus for why the HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-control density override. When null, inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, matches the historical Comfortable default. Only the control height shifts between values.

parameters.name: Block
parameters.description: Stretch the control to fill its container — a full-width input between the −/+ buttons — instead of sizing to its content. Use it in form layouts so the field lines up with sibling full-width inputs/selects (the LumeoForm generator sets it).

parameters.name: ThousandsSeparator

OverlayForm.name: OverlayForm

parameters.name: Validator
parameters.description: Override the default DataAnnotationsValidator with a custom validator (e.g. FluentValidationValidator).

gotchas:
A missing Model no longer fails silently: the EditContext is still
    deferred (EditForm crashes on a null Model + null EditContext), but instead of
    an invisible empty shell the component now renders a dev-visible diagnostic
    so the misconfiguration is obvious during development.

examples.title: Basic Overlay Form
examples.code:
<OverlayForm Model="@_model" OnValidSubmit="HandleSave">
    <Header>
        <Heading Level="3" Class="text-base font-semibold">Edit contact</Heading>
    </Header>
    <Body>
        <Stack Gap="4">
            <Label>Name</Label>
            <Input @bind-Value="_model.Name" Placeholder="Jane Doe" />
            <Label>Email</Label>
            <Input @bind-Value="_model.Email" Placeholder="jane@example.com" />
            <Label>Notes</Label>
            <Textarea @bind-Value="_model.Notes" />
        </Stack>
    </Body>
    <Footer>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Cancel</Button>
        <Button Type="Button.ButtonType.Submit">Save</Button>
    </Footer>
</OverlayForm>

@code {
    private ContactModel _model = new();
    private void HandleSave(EditContext ctx) { /* persist _model */ }
}

examples.title: Prefilled edit + async save
examples.code:
<OverlayForm Model="@_editModel" OnValidSubmit="HandleAsyncSave">
    <Body>
        <Label>Name</Label>
        <Input @bind-Value="_editModel.Name" />
        <Label>Email</Label>
        <Input @bind-Value="_editModel.Email" />
    </Body>
    <Footer>
        <Button Type="Button.ButtonType.Submit" IsLoading="_saving">@(_saving ? "Saving…" : "Save")</Button>
    </Footer>
</OverlayForm>

@code {
    // Prefill from an existing record:
    private ContactModel _editModel = new() { Name = "Ada Lovelace", Email = "ada@example.com" };
    private bool _saving;
    private async Task HandleAsyncSave(EditContext ctx)
    {
        _saving = true;
        await SaveAsync(_editModel);   // await your service
        _saving = false;
    }
}

Pagination.name: Pagination
Pagination.description: Page number bar with prev/next and configurable ranges.

parameters.name: SiblingCount
parameters.description: Pages shown on each side of the current page (default 1).

parameters.name: BoundaryCount
parameters.description: Pages always shown at the very start/end (default 1).

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Pagination>
    <PaginationContent>
        <PaginationPrevious OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(_currentPage - 1))"
                           Disabled="@(_currentPage <= 1)" />
        @for (int i = 1; i <= _totalPages; i++)
        {
            var page = i;
            <PaginationItem IsActive="@(page == _currentPage)"
                           OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(page))">
                @page
            </PaginationItem>
        }
        <PaginationNext OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(_currentPage + 1))"
                       Disabled="@(_currentPage >= _totalPages)" />
    </PaginationContent>
</Pagination>

@code {
    private int _currentPage = 1;
    private int _totalPages = 5;

    private void GoToPage(int page)
    {
        if (page >= 1 && page <= _totalPages)
            _currentPage = page;
    }
}

examples.title: Compact
examples.code:
<Pagination>
    <PaginationContent>
        <PaginationPrevious OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(_currentPage - 1))" Disabled="@(_currentPage <= 1)" />
        <Lumeo.Text As="span" Size="sm" Color="muted" Class="px-3 flex items-center">@_currentPage / @_totalPages</Lumeo.Text>
        <PaginationNext OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(_currentPage + 1))" Disabled="@(_currentPage >= _totalPages)" />
    </PaginationContent>
</Pagination>

examples.title: Size
examples.code:
<Segmented @bind-Value="_paginationSize">
    <SegmentedItem Value="sm">Sm</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="md">Md</SegmentedItem>
    <SegmentedItem Value="lg">Lg</SegmentedItem>
</Segmented>

<Pagination>
    <PaginationContent>
        <PaginationPrevious OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(_currentPage - 1))" Disabled="@(_currentPage <= 1)" />
        @for (int i = 1; i <= 5; i++)
        {
            var page = i;
            <PaginationItem IsActive="@(page == _currentPage)"
                            OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(page))"
                            Class="@(_paginationSize switch { \"sm\" => \"h-7 w-7 text-xs\", \"lg\" => \"h-11 w-11 text-base\", _ => \"\"  })">
                @page
            </PaginationItem>
        }
        <PaginationNext OnClick="@(() => GoToPage(_currentPage + 1))" Disabled="@(_currentPage >= 5)" />
    </PaginationContent>
</Pagination>

@code {
    private string _paginationSize = "md";
    private int _currentPage = 1;
}

parameters.name: FitMode
parameters.description: Fit-to-container mode that overrides Zoom when set to a non-Custom value. FitWidth scales the page so its width matches the scroll-parent's clientWidth (minus a 16px padding for breathing room before the scrollbar). FitPage scales so the whole page fits within the scroll-parent's box. JS computes the ratio on each render, so a container resize that triggers OnParametersSet re-renders the page at the new fit. Set back to Custom to return control to the numeric Zoom value.

parameters.name: OnError
parameters.description: Fires when the underlying JS load/render fails (CDN unreachable, malformed PDF, worker init failure, …). Receives the exception message. Consumers can use this to surface their own error UI; the built-in EmptyState fallback renders regardless.

events.name: OnError
events.description: Fires when the underlying JS load/render fails (CDN unreachable, malformed PDF, worker init failure, …). Receives the exception message. Consumers can use this to surface their own error UI; the built-in EmptyState fallback renders regardless.

gotchas: pdf.js and its worker are loaded at runtime from jsDelivr (CDN) the first time a PdfViewer mounts. For strict-CSP or air-gapped deployments, self-host them and set window.lumeoCdn = { pdfJs: '/your/pdf.min.mjs', pdfJsWorker: '/your/pdf.worker.min.mjs' } before the first PdfViewer renders, or run `lumeo deps install --lib pdfjs` (CLI). See /docs/cdn-deps.

PickList.name: PickList
PickList.description: Two-column shuttle picker — move items between lists.

parameters.name: SourceTitle
parameters.description: Source panel header. Defaults to the localized "Available".

parameters.name: TargetTitle
parameters.description: Target panel header. Defaults to the localized "Selected".

parameters.name: Reorderable
parameters.description: When true (default), the target list can be reordered: header up/down buttons and Alt+ArrowUp/ArrowDown on a focused target item move the selected items within the target order, emitting SelectedItemsChanged. Reorder is gated while a target search is active (a filtered view has no stable order to shuffle).

          TargetTitle="Assigned" />

parameters.name: FocusOnOpen
parameters.description: When true (default), focus moves into the popover surface on open so its @onkeydown handler receives Escape and keyboard users land inside the dialog (the Radix onOpenAutoFocus default). Set to false when the trigger is a typeable control that must KEEP focus while the popover opens (e.g. DatePicker's keyboard-input trigger) — the surface still positions, registers click-outside and saves/restores focus, it just doesn't steal focus from the trigger on open.

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" and wire @onclick="slot.OnClick.InvokeAsync" onto their own single element. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, the trigger renders NO wrapper element and instead cascades a TriggerSlot that a cooperating child folds onto its own single root element — yielding ONE DOM node with merged class, composed click/keyboard, and forwarded ARIA/state (the Radix asChild goal). The child must resolve to exactly one cooperating element (a Lumeo <Button>, or a manual ChildContentSlot splat); raw HTML/3rd-party markup receives nothing. Default false preserves the current role=button wrapper exactly. Prefer a <button> child for a click-toggle trigger: a <Button Href> renders an <a>, which natively activates on Enter only (not Space), so Space would not open the popover.

Progress.name: Progress
Progress.description: Linear progress bar with determinate + indeterminate modes.

parameters.name: Animation
parameters.description: Opt-in animation variant for the linear progress fill. None (default) leaves existing behaviour untouched. Stripe overlays translucent diagonal stripes that sweep across the bar.

enums.name: ProgressAnimation
enums.description: Animation variants for linear progress.

examples.title: Interactive
examples.code:
<Progress Value="@_value" />
<Flex Align="center" Gap="2">
    <Button OnClick="Decrease">-10</Button>
    <Lumeo.Text As="span" Size="sm" Weight="medium">@_value%</Lumeo.Text>
    <Button OnClick="Increase">+10</Button>
</Flex>

@code {
    private int _value = 50;
    private void Increase() => _value = Math.Min(100, _value + 10);

PromptInput.name: PromptInput
PromptInput.description: Multiline AI prompt textarea with submit + keyboard shortcuts.

parameters.name: OnStop
parameters.description: Invoked when the user clicks the Stop button. The Stop button only replaces Send while IsLoading is true AND this callback is bound — so a consumer that doesn't support cancellation keeps the previous disabled-spinner behaviour unchanged.

parameters.name: Attachments
parameters.description: Optional slot for attachment chips/thumbnails, rendered above the textarea.

parameters.name: ShowAttachButton
parameters.description: Shows a built-in paperclip attach button in the leading area that invokes OnAttach.

parameters.name: OnAttach
parameters.description: Invoked when the built-in attach button is clicked (only when ShowAttachButton is true).

parameters.name: MaxLength
parameters.description: Hard character limit. When set, the textarea enforces maxlength and a "count/limit" counter is shown. The counter turns into a destructive color as the limit is approached.

parameters.name: ShowCounter
parameters.description: Forces the character counter to render even when MaxLength is not set (shows the raw count).

events.name: OnStop
events.description: Invoked when the user clicks the Stop button. The Stop button only replaces Send while IsLoading is true AND this callback is bound — so a consumer that doesn't support cancellation keeps the previous disabled-spinner behaviour unchanged.

events.name: OnAttach
events.description: Invoked when the built-in attach button is clicked (only when ShowAttachButton is true).

examples.title: Basic
examples.code:
<PromptInput @bind-Value="_prompt" OnSend="@HandleSend" Placeholder="Ask anything…" />

@code {
    private string? _prompt;
    private void HandleSend(string text) { /* call AI API */ }
}

examples.title: Loading State
examples.code:
<PromptInput @bind-Value="_prompt" IsLoading="@_isLoading" OnSend="@HandleSend" />

@code {
    private string? _prompt;
    private bool _isLoading;

    private async Task HandleSend(string text)
    {
        _isLoading = true;
        await CallAiApi(text);
        _isLoading = false;
    }
}

QueryBuilder.name: QueryBuilder
QueryBuilder.description: Visual AND/OR predicate-tree builder; serializes to JSON or a LINQ predicate.

parameters.name: Fields
parameters.description: The fields the consumer can build conditions against. Required.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The query tree. Two-way bindable via @bind-Query. When null on first render, an empty root group (And, no rules) is created.

parameters.name: ShowJsonPreview
parameters.description: When true, renders a read-only JSON view of the query below the builder.

parameters.name: MaxDepth
parameters.description: Maximum group nesting depth. The "Add group" button is disabled at this depth.

parameters.name: AllowEmptyGroups
parameters.description: When false, "Remove rule" / "Remove group" are hidden when it would leave a group empty (the root may always be empty).

parameters.name: ValueEditorTemplate
parameters.description: Optional override for how a rule's value editor renders. Receives the rule. When supplied, replaces the built-in per-type editor entirely (the consumer is then responsible for binding to Value and calling NotifyChanged via the cascaded context — see docs).

records.name: QueryBuilderContext
records.signature:
QueryBuilderContext(
        IReadOnlyList<QueryField> Fields,
        int MaxDepth,
        bool AllowEmptyGroups,
        RenderFragment<QueryRule>? ValueEditorTemplate,
        Func<Task> NotifyChanged)
records.description: Cascaded state shared with the recursive QueryBuilderGroup renderer.

examples.title: Basic builder
examples.code:
<QueryBuilder Fields="_fields" @bind-Query="_query" />

@code {
    private readonly IReadOnlyList<QueryField> _fields = new List<QueryField>
    {
        new() { Name = "name", Label = "Name", Type = QueryFieldType.Text },
        new() { Name = "age", Label = "Age", Type = QueryFieldType.Number },
        new() { Name = "isActive", Label = "Active", Type = QueryFieldType.Boolean },
        new() { Name = "plan", Label = "Plan", Type = QueryFieldType.Select,
                Options = new[] { ("free", "Free"), ("pro", "Pro") } }
    };

    private QueryGroup _query = QueryGroup.CreateEmpty();
}

examples.title: With JSON preview
examples.code:
<QueryBuilder Fields="_fields" @bind-Query="_query" ShowJsonPreview="true" />

@code {
    // ShowJsonPreview renders the live System.Text.Json shape — handy for
    // showing the consumer exactly what would be sent to a backend, or for debugging.
    // Programmatically: var json = QueryBuilder.ToJson(_query);
    //                   var back = QueryBuilder.FromJson(json);
}

examples.title: Pre-populated nested groups
examples.code:
<QueryBuilder Fields="_fields" @bind-Query="_query" MaxDepth="3" />

@code {
    private QueryGroup _query = new()
    {
        Combinator = QueryCombinator.And,
        Rules =
        {
            new QueryRule { Field = "isActive", Operator = "=", Value = true },
            new QueryGroup
            {
                Combinator = QueryCombinator.Or,
                Rules =
                {
                    new QueryRule { Field = "plan", Operator = "=", Value = "pro" },
                    new QueryRule { Field = "plan", Operator = "=", Value = "enterprise" }
                }
            }
        }
    };

    // Run it client-side:
    // var predicate = QueryBuilder.ToExpression<Customer>(_query);
    // var matches = customers.Where(predicate.Compile());
}

parameters.description: The parent group (null for the root) — used to support self-removal.

ReasoningDisplay.name: ReasoningDisplay
ReasoningDisplay.description: Collapsible chain-of-thought block for AI reasoning traces.

parameters.name: Markdown
parameters.description: Renders Text as markdown instead of plain text — reasoning traces from models are usually markdown (lists, bold, code). Uses MarkdownRenderer when supplied, otherwise the built-in dependency-free CommonMark subset. Pairs naturally with Detailed. (#305)

parameters.name: MarkdownRenderer
parameters.description: Optional render hook converting the raw markdown Text into a MarkupString (plug in Markdig etc.). The delegate must return SAFE HTML — sanitise untrusted input. Ignored unless Markdown is true.

enums.name: ReasoningVariant
enums.description: Default = standard expand/collapse display. Compact = summary label + collapsed body limited to 3 lines. Detailed = always shows full body text with extra padding.

parameters.name: Collapsible
parameters.description: When true, the panel can collapse to CollapsedSize — by dragging its handle below (roughly) half the MinSize, or via the group's ToggleCollapse from the cascading context. Restores on drag-out / toggle. (#257)

parameters.name: CollapsedSize
parameters.description: Percentage the panel occupies while collapsed. Default 0. Only meaningful when Collapsible is true.

parameters.name: SavedLayout
parameters.description: Optional previously-persisted panel sizes. The group seeds its layout from this on first render (taking precedence over panel DefaultSizes and DefaultSizes). Mirrors DataGrid / Tabs SavedLayout: the library never persists on its own — store the snapshot you receive from OnLayout and feed it back here on init.

parameters.name: OnLayout
parameters.description: Fires after every resize (drag, keyboard step, or collapse toggle) with the current panel sizes so the consumer can persist them. Pairs with SavedLayout to round-trip the layout through storage.

events.name: OnLayout
events.description: Fires after every resize (drag, keyboard step, or collapse toggle) with the current panel sizes so the consumer can persist them. Pairs with SavedLayout to round-trip the layout through storage.

records.name: ResizablePanelGroupContext

ResizablePanelGroupContext(

        EventCallback<(int PanelIndex, double Delta)> OnHandleDrag,

        int PanelCount,
        Action<int, double, double, double, bool, double> RegisterPanel,
        Func<int, (double MinSize, double MaxSize)> GetPanelConstraints,
        Func<int, bool> IsPanelCollapsed,
        EventCallback<int> ToggleCollapse,
        Action<int> UnregisterPanel
    )

parameters.name: ToolbarMode
parameters.description: Legacy toolbar mode; superseded by Toolbar.

parameters.name: Toolbar
parameters.description: Toolbar density preset. Defaults to Standard.

parameters.name: MaxWordImportBytes
parameters.description: Maximum size, in bytes, of a Word document accepted by the in-browser import path. Defaults to 8 MB. Larger files are refused before being read into memory and surfaced to the user via WordImportError. Set explicitly when consumers expect large documents and the deployment can afford the memory.

parameters.name: OnImageRequested
parameters.description: Resolves a picked image into a URL (or data URI). Receives file metadata from JS; consumer is expected to upload the file out-of-band (e.g. via a paired InputFile) and return a final URL. If null, images fall back to base64.

parameters.name: OnAiAction
parameters.description: Selection-driven AI actions. Receives the action id and selected plain text; returns replacement HTML to be inserted in place of the selection.

parameters.name: OnWordImportRequested
parameters.description: Optional override for Word import. Receives the picked file payload (filename + bytes) and returns HTML to replace the editor content. When NOT set, the file is converted in-browser via WordImporter (Mammoth runs inside the WASM bundle), so the demo works on static hosting with no server. Set this when you want server-side conversion (better fidelity for complex docs).

parameters.name: NowIndicator
parameters.description: Show the current-time indicator line in time-grid views. Default: true. Maps to FullCalendar's nowIndicator option.

parameters.name: SlotMinTime
parameters.description: Earliest visible time slot in time-grid views (e.g. 07:00). Maps to FullCalendar's slotMinTime. Default: 00:00 (FullCalendar shows the full day).

parameters.name: SlotMaxTime
parameters.description: Latest visible time slot in time-grid views (e.g. 22:00). Maps to FullCalendar's slotMaxTime. Default: 24:00 (FullCalendar shows the full day).

parameters.name: SlotDuration
parameters.description: Duration of each time slot row in time-grid views. Maps to FullCalendar's slotDuration (formatted as HH:mm:ss). Default: 00:30:00.

parameters.name: Resources
parameters.description: Optional list of resources (people, rooms, equipment). When provided, events are color-coded by their ResourceId and a legend is rendered above the calendar. Note: this uses Lumeo's color-coding fallback, NOT FullCalendar Premium resource lanes (which require a paid FullCalendar Premium license not bundled here).

parameters.name: EventClassNames
parameters.description: Optional callback that returns extra CSS class names for an event chip. The classes are appended to the FullCalendar event's classNames. Use for conditional styling without touching Color.

parameters.name: EventColor
parameters.description: Optional callback that returns a CSS color string for an event. Applied as backgroundColor / borderColor in FullCalendar. Supersedes Color when both are set; resource color applies as a fallback when neither is set.

parameters.name: OnInitError
parameters.description: Raised when JS initialization fails (e.g. CDN down, FullCalendar threw). The argument is the error message. When raised the component renders an error card instead of the calendar.

events.name: OnInitError
events.description: Raised when JS initialization fails (e.g. CDN down, FullCalendar threw). The argument is the error message. When raised the component renders an error card instead of the calendar.

<Dialog @bind-Open="_open">

        <DialogFooter>
            <Button OnClick="Add">Add event</Button>
        </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

@code {
    private bool _open;
    private SchedulerDateRange? _range;
    private string _title = "";
    private List<SchedulerEvent> _events = new();

    void HandleDateSelect(SchedulerDateRange r) { _range = r; _open = true; }
    void Add() {
        _events = _events.Append(new SchedulerEvent(
            Id: Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N"),
            Title: _title,
            Start: _range!.Start,
            End: _range.End,
            AllDay: _range.AllDay
        )).ToList();
        _open = false;
    }
}

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: When the scrollbars become visible. Mirrors Radix UI's ScrollArea type: Auto (default) — bars show only when the content overflows. Always — bars are always visible and the gutter is reserved (no layout shift when content grows). Scroll — bars show while scrolling. Hover — bars show on hover / focus within. Implemented cross-browser via the lumeo-scrollarea CSS (Firefox scrollbar-* + WebKit pseudo-elements). Firefox exposes only a thin bar, so the Hover/Scroll auto-hide granularity is a WebKit/overlay enhancement that degrades gracefully to a persistent thin bar there.

parameters.name: FocusRingGutter
parameters.description: Adds a 4px inline gutter (-mx-1 px-1, net-zero layout) so a focusable control's focus ring isn't clipped at the left/right edge. Per CSS spec overflow-auto on one axis promotes the other to auto too, so a full-width <Input> flush against the scroll box loses its box-shadow ring on that edge. Opt-in (default false) because the negative margin makes the element 4px wider per side — only enable when the scroll area holds focusable controls (forms, menus), not when it wraps edge-aligned content like a table.

enums.name: ScrollAreaType
enums.description: Scrollbar visibility strategy. See Type.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: Data-driven options. When set, takes precedence over ChildContent. Leave null and pass SegmentedItem children for the compound-component flavour.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Compound-mode content — one or more SegmentedItem. Ignored when Options is set.

parameters.name: Density
parameters.description: Per-control density override. When null, inherits from any ancestor DensityScope; if neither is set, matches the historical Comfortable default. Only the track + item padding shift between values.

records.name: SegmentedItemEntry
records.signature: SegmentedItemEntry(string Value, string ItemId, bool Disabled)
records.description: One entry in the ordered segment registry: the segment's current , its STABLE (the key for every registry mutation — survives a Value change) and its current state.

records.name: SegmentedContext
records.signature:
SegmentedContext(
        string? Value,
        EventCallback<string> Select,
        Func<bool, string> GetItemClasses,
        Func<string, bool, string> GetTabIndex,
        IReadOnlyList<SegmentedItemEntry> Items,
        Action<string, string, bool> RegisterItem,
        Action<string, string, string, bool> ReplaceItem,
        Action<string> UnregisterItem,
        // The role=radiogroup element id — the anchor for the DOM-order roving-nav
        // probe (Interop.GetOrderedDescendantIds) so arrow-key order tracks the live
        // DOM after a keyed reorder / middle-insert of SegmentedItem children.
        string ContainerId)
records.description: Cascading context for compound-mode SegmentedItem children — they read the active value + the parent's item-class function (so style stays consistent with Options-mode), register / re-key / unregister themselves in the shared ordered registry (for roving tabindex + arrow navigation) keyed by their stable ItemId, and invoke Select on click.

examples.code: <Segmented @bind-Value="_value" Options="_options" />

examples.code: <Segmented @bind-Value="_value" Options="_options" Block="true" />

examples.title: With Disabled Option
examples.code: new SegmentedOption { Label = "Weekly", Value = "weekly", Disabled = true }

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value selected when this item is clicked. Must match one of the parent Segmented's expected values.

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Optional leading icon slot.

Select.name: Select
Select.description: Native-feeling styled dropdown with search and groups.

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Popover open state. Bind via @bind-Open when the Select lives inside a parent that re-renders heavily (e.g. an EditForm with active validation) — otherwise a parameter cascade can close the popover mid-click. In the bound (controlled) mode the parent owns state and the popover survives re-renders. When unbound, the component still functions but is vulnerable to the cascade-close race; binding is the recommended pattern.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Custom CSS classes merged onto the Select's wrapper element. Consistent with every other form component per the library's base API contract.

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: Convenience placeholder shown by SelectTrigger when no value is selected and the trigger doesn't set its own Placeholder. Lets consumers write <Select Placeholder="…"> instead of threading it through the trigger — matching DatePicker / TimePicker / Combobox's top-level placeholder ergonomics.

parameters.name: AutoFocus
parameters.description: Focuses the select's trigger button on first render. See Input.AutoFocus — the HTML autofocus attribute doesn't work in Blazor WASM, this parameter uses FocusElement after mount.

parameters.name: Items
parameters.description: Optional data source. When set, SelectContent renders options from this list instead of ChildContent.

parameters.name: ItemValue
parameters.description: Extracts the string value for each item. Defaults to ToString().

parameters.name: ItemText
parameters.description: Extracts the display label for each item. Defaults to ItemValue when null.

parameters.name: ItemGroup
parameters.description: Optional group key extractor. When set, options are grouped under sticky headers.

parameters.name: ItemDisabled
parameters.description: Optional per-item disabled predicate.

parameters.name: ItemDescription
parameters.description: Optional per-item secondary line (description) extractor. When set and ItemTemplate is null, the default item renderer shows the description in a smaller, muted line below the label.

parameters.name: ItemIcon
parameters.description: Optional per-item leading icon. Receives the raw item object and returns a RenderFragment (typically a single <Icon> or <SvgGlyph>). Rendered before the label when ItemTemplate is null. Return null per item to mix iconed and non-iconed rows.

parameters.name: ItemTemplate
parameters.description: Optional custom render fragment for an item's content. Receives the raw item object.

parameters.name: Virtualize
parameters.description: When true and Items is set, uses Blazor's Virtualize component for the option list.

parameters.name: ItemSize
parameters.description: Estimated pixel height of each option row used by the Virtualize component. Default 36.

records.name: SelectContext
records.signature:
SelectContext(
        string? Value, bool IsOpen, bool Searchable, string SearchText,
        bool Multiple, List<string>? Values, bool IsLoading, bool Disabled, bool Clearable, bool Creatable, int MaxDisplayTags,
        EventCallback<string> OnSelect, EventCallback<bool> SetOpen, EventCallback<string> OnSearch,
        EventCallback OnClear, EventCallback<string> OnCreateItem,
        string WrapperId, string ContentId, string TriggerId, int FocusedIndex, string? FocusedItemId, string? FocusedItemValue,
        EventCallback<int> SetFocusedIndex,
        EventCallback<string> OnTypeahead,
        Action<string, string, string, bool, RenderFragment?> RegisterItem, Action<string> UnregisterItem,
        bool Invalid, bool Required,
        // Data-bound mode params (null when composition mode)
        IEnumerable<object>? Items,
        Func<object, string> ItemValue,
        Func<object, string>? ItemText,
        Func<object, string?>? ItemGroup,
        Func<object, bool>? ItemDisabled,
        Func<object, string?>? ItemDescription,
        Func<object, RenderFragment?>? ItemIcon,
        RenderFragment<object>? ItemTemplate,
        bool Virtualize,
        float ItemSize)

gotchas: Data-bound mode (the Items parameter) still requires <SelectTrigger /> and <SelectContent /> children — a bare <Select Items="..." /> with no children shows only an empty field. Map items with ItemValue/ItemText; items are typed as object, so the data-bound Select is not generic.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: Group header text. When null/empty no header renders (still nests its items).

parameters.name: LabelClass
parameters.description: Extra classes for the group label — theming hook for the header.

parameters.name: Collapsible
parameters.description: When true the header toggles the group open/closed. Collapsed groups hide their items.

parameters.name: DefaultExpanded
parameters.description: Initial expanded state for a Collapsible group. Default expanded.

parameters.name: GroupSelect
parameters.description: In Multiple mode, render a tri-state checkbox on the header that selects / deselects every descendant item in one click. Disabled descendants are skipped.

parameters.name: SearchValue
parameters.description: Optional text used to match against the search input when Searchable is enabled. Falls back to Value.

parameters.name: Decorative
parameters.description: When true (the default), the separator is purely visual and exposes role="none" with no orientation — assistive tech skips it, matching Radix's decorative default. Set to false for a semantic separator that conveys structure (e.g. between menu groups or list sections): the element then carries role="separator" plus an aria-orientation matching Orientation. The implicit ARIA orientation of a separator is horizontal, so a vertical one must declare it explicitly.

parameters.name: Inset
parameters.description: When true, the rule is inset from the container edges (a 0.75rem margin on the long axis: mx-3 when horizontal, my-3 when vertical) instead of spanning the full length — the Material / MudBlazor "inset divider" look, softer than an edge-to-edge line. Default false.

events.name: OnBeforeClose
events.description: Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape, backdrop click, swipe gesture, X button, or a SheetClose trigger). Set Cancel to true to abort. The Reason tells you which path fired ("escape", "outside", "swipe", "close").

parameters.name: SizePreset
parameters.description: Alternative to Size that accepts the unified Size token. When set (non-null), wins over Size. Mapping: Sm→Sm, Md→Default, Lg→Lg, Xl→Xl. Full has no Size equivalent (Full is a layout intent, not a scale) — set Size directly for that case. Closes the Lumeo.Size ↔ per-component-enum overlap from #87.2.

parameters.name: Animation
parameters.description: How the sheet enters/leaves. Slide (default) animates from the configured Side. Fade uses a simple opacity tween — useful in dense forms where popovers and animations compete. None disables the animation entirely.

parameters.name: ShowCloseButton
parameters.description: Whether the top-end close (X) button renders. null (default) keeps the legacy coupling — X shows whenever PreventClose is false. true forces the X on a modal sheet (backdrop/Escape stay disabled; the X routes through the dismiss guard); false hides it. Mirrors OverlayOptions.ShowCloseButton for service sheets.

parameters.name: SwipeToClose
parameters.description: When true, the sheet can be dismissed by swiping in the direction opposite to its Side (e.g. swipe-down on a Bottom sheet). Default false to avoid conflicts with scrollable content. Wire to true only when the sheet's content fits without scrolling.

parameters.name: ZIndex
parameters.description: Backdrop z-index. Content sits at ZIndex + 1. Defaults to BaseZIndex; OverlayProvider supplies an incrementing value per nested overlay.

enums.name: SheetSize
enums.description: Sheet sizes. Full is layout-intent (entire viewport), not a scale value, so this enum is intentionally NOT replaced by Size in 3.0.

gotchas: No inner scroll container by default — the panel is a flex-col, so a long body overflows the viewport. Wrap the scrollable region in flex-1 overflow-y-auto, or use OverlayForm which bakes that in.

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary markup — receives the TriggerSlot to splat manually. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, renders NO wrapper and cascades a TriggerSlot a cooperating child (a Lumeo <Button>, or a manual ChildContentSlot) folds onto its own single root element — the Radix asChild goal: one DOM node, no nested-interactive markup. Prefer a <button> child (a <Button Href> link activates on Enter only, not Space). Default false keeps the current role=button wrapper.

ShimmerButton.description: Button with animated shimmer border beam.

parameters.name: ShimmerColor
parameters.description: CSS color used for the shimmer sweep — defaults to a translucent foreground.

parameters.name: PressEffect
parameters.description: Visual feedback when the button is pressed down. Default is None (no press animation — original behaviour).

parameters.description: Render the bottom divider. Default true. Set false for a seamless header that flows into the content without a rule — replaces the consumer-side !border-b-0 override.

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Icon slot. Named IconContent to avoid shadowing the <Icon> component.

parameters.name: LabelContent
parameters.description: Label slot. Named LabelContent to avoid shadowing the <Label> component.

parameters.name: Tooltip
parameters.description: Hint shown via the Lumeo <Tooltip> when the rail is collapsed (icon-only). Renders as a styled, accessible tooltip — not the native browser title — and stays hidden while the rail is expanded (the label is visible then).

parameters.name: MobileSheet
parameters.description: When true, the sidebar renders as an off-canvas sheet (slide-in over a dismissible scrim) on mobile viewports (< md), regardless of Variant. On larger viewports it keeps its configured variant. Collapsed acts as the sheet's closed state; toggling opens it. Opt-in (default false) so existing layouts are unchanged. (#241)

parameters.name: ToggleShortcut
parameters.description: Keyboard shortcut that toggles the sidebar, in the IKeyboardShortcutService combo format (e.g. "ctrl+b", "meta+b"). Registered on first render and released on dispose. Defaults to "ctrl+b" which — matching shadcn's (metaKey || ctrlKey) && key === "b" — fires on both Ctrl+B and Cmd+B. Override with a different combo, or set to null/empty to disable the shortcut entirely.

parameters.name: PersistCollapsed
parameters.description: When true, the collapsed / expanded state survives page reloads — the provider reads IsCollapsed from localStorage[PersistenceKey] on first render and writes back on every change. Falls back to the parameter value when JS is unavailable (SSR, prerender, JSDisconnectedException). Opt-in to keep existing consumers' behaviour untouched.

parameters.name: PersistenceKey
parameters.description: LocalStorage key used when PersistCollapsed is true. Override to scope a per-route or per-tenant sidebar so multiple sidebars on the same origin don't clobber each other's state.

enums.name: SidebarVariant
enums.description: Layout strategy for the sidebar. Push — collapses to zero width, pushing main content to the edge. Overlay — floats above content with a dismissible scrim. Icon — collapses to a narrow icon rail that stays put until toggled back. MiniRail — same icon-rail width when collapsed, but expands to full width on hover or keyboard focus and collapses again on leave. Material’s standard collapsed-sidebar pattern; uses Toggle() to pin open.

records.name: SidebarState
records.signature:
SidebarState(
        bool IsCollapsed,
        EventCallback Toggle,
        SidebarVariant Variant)

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: Export format for the emitted data URL. "png" is the default (universal browser support, transparent backgrounds). "svg" produces a real vector export: the JS pad records each pointer path and replays it as <path> elements, so the signature scales crisply at any size. (A signature loaded from an existing raster data URL has no vector data to export and falls back to PNG.)

examples.title: Basic
examples.code:
<SignaturePad @bind-Value="_signature" />

@@if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(_signature))
{

}

@@code {
    private string? _signature;
}

@@code {
    private string _savedSignature = "data:image/png;base64,...";
}

Slider.name: Slider
Slider.description: Range slider with single and dual thumb modes.

parameters.name: MinStepsBetweenThumbs
parameters.description: Minimum permitted distance between the two range thumbs, expressed in Step units (so the absolute gap is MinStepsBetweenThumbs * Step). Mirrors Radix's minStepsBetweenThumbs. The default of 0 lets the thumbs meet but — unlike before — they can no longer cross past each other. Only meaningful when IsRange is true.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible name exposed via aria-label on the underlying range input. Required for WCAG compliance when the slider is not visually labelled by adjacent text connected via for/htmlFor. For range sliders (IsRange=true) the end handle's aria-label is automatically suffixed with "(end)".

parameters.name: Culture
parameters.description: Culture used for the default tooltip/mark value formatting. When null (default) falls back to CurrentCulture. Ignored when FormatTooltip is supplied.

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Slider @bind-Value="_value" />
<Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Value: @_value</Lumeo.Text>

@code {
    private double _value = 50;
}

examples.title: Custom Range
examples.code:

@code {
    private double _value = 500;
}

examples.title: Step Values
examples.code:
<Slider @bind-Value="_value" Min="0" Max="10" Step="1" />

@code {
    private double _value = 5;
}

examples.title: Disabled
examples.code: <Slider Value="40" Disabled="true" />

parameters.name: Group
parameters.description: Optional shared-group name. When set, it is emitted on the container as a data-sortable-group attribute so groups are observable in the DOM. NOTE: cross-list transfer between lists that share a group is NOT yet implemented — the attribute is reserved for that future capability and current behaviour is intra-list reordering only.

parameters.name: OnReorder
parameters.description: Raised in addition to ItemsChanged whenever an item is reordered (via drag, touch, or keyboard), carrying the moved item along with its old and new indices. Does not change the ItemsChanged contract.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: Optional selector that returns a stable, unique key for each item. Providing this ensures Blazor's diffing engine tracks items by identity rather than index — critical for drag-reorder to map DOM state correctly. If omitted, the item reference itself is used as the key.

events.name: OnReorder
events.description: Raised in addition to ItemsChanged whenever an item is reordered (via drag, touch, or keyboard), carrying the moved item along with its old and new indices. Does not change the ItemsChanged contract.

records.name: SortableMoved
records.signature: SortableMoved(TItem Item, int OldIndex, int NewIndex)
records.description: Payload describing a single reorder: the moved and its / within the list. Supplied to OnReorder.

examples.title: Basic Sortable List
examples.code:
<SortableList TItem="string" Items="@_tasks" ItemsChanged="@(v => _tasks = v)">
    <ItemTemplate Context="item">
        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm">@item</Lumeo.Text>
    </ItemTemplate>
</SortableList>

</SortableList>

            <Stack Gap="0" Class="flex-1">
                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm font-medium">@task.Title</Lumeo.Text>
                <Lumeo.Text Class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">@task.Assignee</Lumeo.Text>
            </Stack>
            <Badge Variant="Badge.BadgeVariant.Secondary">@task.Priority</Badge>
        </Flex>
    </ItemTemplate>
</SortableList>

        <Lumeo.Text Class="text-sm">@item</Lumeo.Text>
    </ItemTemplate>
</SortableList>

Spacer.name: Spacer
Spacer.description: Flex-grow spacer that pushes siblings apart.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Tailwind size token (e.g. "4", "px"). When set, renders a fixed-size square spacer. When unset, renders a flex-grow spacer that fills available space along the parent flex axis.

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.description: Hints which axis the parent flex container uses so the grow-spacer collapses to zero on the cross-axis instead of expanding it. Default Horizontal keeps the historical behaviour (flex-1 inside a row). Switch to Vertical when the parent is a column stack — without it, the spacer's flex-1 took width from the column instead of pushing siblings to opposite ends.

examples.title: Flex Spacer
examples.code:
<Flex Align="center">
    <Text Weight="semibold">Brand</Text>
    <Spacer />
    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">Login</Button>
</Flex>

examples.code:

    <Spacer Size="4" />
    <Stack Gap="0">

        <Text Color="muted" Size="xs">jane@example.com</Text>
    </Stack>
</Stack>

examples.title: Vertical Grow Spacer
examples.code:
<Stack Direction="Lumeo.Orientation.Vertical" Class="h-48">
    <Text Weight="semibold">Header</Text>
    <Spacer Orientation="Lumeo.Orientation.Vertical" />
    <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" Size="Button.ButtonSize.Sm">Footer action</Button>
</Stack>

parameters.description: Override the "Finish" button label. When null, uses the localized L["Stepper.Finish"]. (#245)

records.name: StepperContext

StepperContext(

        bool Linear,

        Stepper Parent,

    </StepperStep>
</Stepper>

StreamingText.name: StreamingText
StreamingText.description: Token-by-token streaming text renderer for AI responses.

parameters.name: Prose
parameters.description: Applies Tailwind Typography (prose) styling to the rendered output. Meaningful on its own only when Markdown (or a consumer's own markup via MarkdownRenderer) produces real block/inline elements for the prose styles to target.

parameters.name: Markdown
parameters.description: Renders Text as markdown instead of plain text. Uses MarkdownRenderer when supplied, otherwise a built-in dependency-free CommonMark subset (headings, lists, bold/italic, inline + fenced code, links, blockquotes). Incremental fade-in is disabled in this mode because markdown re-parses the whole string each update.

parameters.name: MarkdownRenderer
parameters.description: Optional render hook: converts the raw markdown Text into a MarkupString. Supply this to plug in a full renderer (Markdig, etc.). The delegate is responsible for producing SAFE HTML — sanitise untrusted input before returning. Ignored unless Markdown is true.
